blocks:
Chapter At-Tawbah

Medinan, and it is 129 verses

(١) **بَراءَةٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ إِلَى الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتُمْ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (1) A declaration of immunity from Allah and His Apostle to those of the polytheists with whom you had made a treaty.

Meaning: this is a declaration of immunity (بَراءَةٌ). And the meaning is that Allah and His Apostle are absolved (بريئان) from the covenant (العهد) with which عاهَدْتُمْ ** (you had made a treaty) with الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (the polytheists). If it is asked how it is permissible for the Prophet (النّبيّ) to break the covenant (العهد), the answer is given in two ways.

The first is that he had stipulated to them the continuation of the covenant until Allah should lift it by revelation (بوحي). And the second is that they had already broken it or intended to do so, so Allah commanded that their covenant be broken.

And in al-Majma', he attributed the two ways (الوجهين) to the narration (الرّواية).

(٢) فَسِيحُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ ** (So travel in the land for four months) This is an address to the polytheists (المشركين). They were commanded to travel in the land for four months, safe wherever they wished, without being exposed to harm, and then they would be killed wherever they were found.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): So Allah granted a respite to the polytheists who performed the Hajj that year for four months, until they returned to their places of safety, and then they would be killed wherever they were found.

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed after the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم returned from the Battle of Tabuk in the ninth year of the Hijra. He said: When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم conquered Mecca, He did not prevent the polytheists from performing Hajj that year. It was a custom (سنة) among the Arabs during Hajj that whoever entered Mecca and circumambulated the House (البيت) in his clothes was not permitted to keep them; they would give them away in charity and not wear them after the circumambulation (الطّواف). Thus, whoever arrived in Mecca would borrow a garment and circumambulate in it, then return it. Whoever could not find a borrowed garment would rent clothes. And whoever could not find a borrowed garment or rent, and had only one garment, would circumambulate the House naked. A beautiful and attractive woman from the Arabs came and sought a borrowed garment or rent, but did not find it. They said to her: "If you circumambulate in your clothes, you will be obliged to give them away in charity." She said: "How can I give them away in charity when I have no other?" So she circumambulated the House naked, and people looked upon her. She placed one hand over her front and the other over her back and said: "Today, some of it or all of it appears, and whatever appears of it, I do not make it lawful (فلا أحلّه)." When she finished the circumambulation, a group of men proposed marriage to her, but she said: "I have a husband." The practice (سيرة) of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم before the revelation of Surat Bara'ah was not to fight except those who fought Him, nor to wage war except against those who waged war against Him and sought Him. And concerning this, the Almighty and Majestic Allah had revealed to Him: فَإِنِ اعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقاتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ فَما جَعَلَ اللهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلاً ** (But if they keep out of your way and do not fight you, and offer you peace, then Allah has not given you any way against them.) Thus, the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم would not fight anyone who withdrew from Him and kept away, until Surat Bara'ah was revealed to Him, commanding Him to kill the polytheists, whether they withdrew from Him or did not withdraw, except for those whom the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم had made a treaty with on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca for a specific period, among them Safwan ibn Umayyah and Suhayl ibn Amr. So the Almighty and Majestic Allah said: بَراءَةٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ إِلَى الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتُمْ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ فَسِيحُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ ** (A repudiation from Allah and His Apostle to those of the polytheists with whom you have made a treaty. So travel in the land for four months). Then they would be killed wherever they were found. These months of travel (السياحة) are twenty days of Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, Safar, the month of Rabi' al-Awwal, and ten days of Rabi' al-Akhir.

When the verses from the beginning of Bara'ah were revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم handed them to Abu Bakr and commanded him to go out to Mecca and recite them to the people at Mina on the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر). When Abu Bakr departed, Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, no one shall convey on your behalf except a man from you." So the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم sent the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in pursuit of him. He overtook him at al-Rawha' (1) and took the verses from him. Abu Bakr returned to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, was something revealed concerning me?" He said: "Indeed, Allah commanded Me that no one should convey on My behalf except Me or a man from Me."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Conquest (الفتح) was in the eighth year, Bara'ah was in the ninth year, and the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجّة الوداع) was in the tenth year.

And from him (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم sent Abu Bakr with Bara'ah to the season (الموسم) to recite it to the people. Then Gabriel descended and said: "No one shall convey on your behalf except Ali (peace be upon him)." So the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم called Ali and commanded him to ride His she-camel al-Adbā' (2) and commanded him to overtake Abu Bakr, take al-Bara'ah from him, and recite it to the people in Mecca. Abu Bakr asked: "Did He become angry with me?" He replied: "No, but it was revealed to Him that no one should convey it except a man from you." When Ali (peace be upon him) arrived in Mecca, it was the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر) after noon, which is the Day of the Greater Pilgrimage (يَوْمَ الْحَجِّ الْأَكْبَرِ). He stood up and then said: "I am the messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) to you," and he recited to them: بَراءَةٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ إِلَى الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتُمْ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ فَسِيحُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ ** (A repudiation from Allah and His Apostle to those of the polytheists with whom you have made a treaty. So travel in the land for four months) — twenty days of Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, Safar, the month of Rabi' al-Awwal, and ten days of Rabi' al-Akhir. He said: "No naked man or woman, nor any polytheist, shall circumambulate the House, except for those who had a treaty with the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, whose term extends to these four months."

He said: And in the narration of Muhammad ibn Muslim: Abu Bakr said: "O Ali, has anything been revealed concerning me since I left the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم?" He replied: "No, but Allah refused that anyone should convey on behalf of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم except a man from Him." So he arrived at the season (الموسم) and conveyed the message from Allah and His Messenger at Arafat, Muzdalifah, and on the Day of Sacrifice near the Jimār (stone pillars), throughout all the Days of Tashrīq, proclaiming: **بَراءَةٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** (A repudiation from Allah and His Apostle) — the verse — and saying: "And let no naked person circumambulate the House."

And in al-Majma', our companions narrated: That the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم also appointed him (ولّاه) over the season (الموسم), and that when he took al-Bara'ah from Abu Bakr, Abu Bakr returned.

And in it (al-Majma') and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: Ali (peace be upon him) addressed the people and drew his sword, saying: "No naked person shall circumambulate the House, nor shall any polytheist perform the Hajj of the House. And whoever has a term [of treaty], his term is until its end, and whoever does not have a term, his term is four months." He delivered the sermon on the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر), so the term was twenty days of Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, Safar, the month of Rabi' al-Awwal, and ten days of Rabi' al-Akhir. وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِي اللهِ ** (And know that you cannot frustrate Allah) — you cannot escape Him, even if He grants you respite — وَأَنَّ اللهَ مُخْزِي الْكافِرِينَ ** (and that Allah will disgrace the faithless) — He will humiliate them through killing and captivity in this world, and punishment in the Hereafter.

(١) Al-Rawha' (الرّوحاء) is a place between the Two Sanctuaries (الحرمين), thirty or forty miles from Medina.

(٢) In the Hadith: Do not sacrifice al-Adbā' (العَضباء with madd), which is one whose inner horn is broken or whose ear is slit, as mentioned in al-Mughrib and others. Al-Adbā' (العضباء) is the name of a she-camel that belonged to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. It is said that it was a proper name for her, and it is also said that her ear was slit. In the words of al-Zamakhshari, it is transmitted from their saying: Nāqatun Adbā' (ناقة عضباء) is the short-handed she-camel.

(٣) وَأَذانٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ إِلَى النَّاسِ ** (3) and an announcement from Allah and His Apostle to the people An announcement (إيذان) and a declaration (إعلام), and it is like the granting of security (الأمان) and the giving (العطاء), in the sense of faith (الإيمان) and aggression (الاعتداء). يَوْمَ الْحَجِّ الْأَكْبَرِ ** on the day of the greater pilgrimage. It is said to be the Day of al-Eid (the Festival), because in it is the completion of the Hajj and the majority of its rites, and because the declaration (الإعلام) took place on it.

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The announcement (الأذان) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). Al-Qummi narrated, and in another hadith, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "I was the announcement (الأذان) among the people." The latter is narrated in al-Ma'ani and al-Ilal from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and they added: He was asked: "What is the meaning of this phrase: الْحَجِّ الْأَكْبَرِ ** (the greater pilgrimage)?" He replied: "It was only named the Greater (الأكبر) because it was the year in which the Muslims and the polytheists performed Hajj, and the polytheists did not perform Hajj after that year."

And in al-Kafi, al-Ma'ani, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in several reports: يَوْمَ الْحَجِّ الْأَكْبَرِ ** (the day of the greater pilgrimage) is the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النّحر). The lesser (الأصغر) is the Umrah.

And in some reports of al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): الْحَجِّ الْأَكْبَرِ ** (the greater pilgrimage) is the standing at Arafah (الوقوف بعرفة) and the stoning of the Jamarat (رمي الجمار). And the lesser Hajj (الحجّ الأصغر) is the Umrah. And al-Ayyashi added: and Jam' (جَمْع) (1) after Arafah.

أَنَّ اللهَ بَرِيءٌ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَرَسُولُهُ ** that Allah and His Apostle are quit of the polytheists. It is an atf (conjunction) upon the pronoun in بَرِيءٌ (quit/free). There is no repetition in it, because the first part was an announcement of the establishment of the disavowal (البراءة), and this is an announcement declaring it to the people. فَإِنْ تُبْتُمْ ** If you repent, from disbelief (الكفر) and treachery (الغدر), فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَإِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ ** that will be better for you, but if you turn your backs, from repentance (التّوبة), فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِي اللهِ ** know that you cannot frustrate Allah. you will not precede Allah, nor will you escape His might (بأسه) and His punishment (عذابه). وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** And inform the faithless of a painful punishment in the Hereafter (الآخِرة).

(1) And Jam' (جَمْع) (with fathah then sukūn) is al-Mash'ar al-Haram (المشعر الحرام), and it is the closer of the two standing places (الموقفين) to Mecca the Honored (مكّة المشرّفة). And from this is the hadith of Adam (peace be upon him): "Then he reached Jam' (جَمْع), and he combined the sunset (المغرب) and evening (العشاء) prayers therein." It is said it was named thus because people gather (يجتمعون) there and draw near (يزدلفون) to Allah, the Exalted, meaning they seek proximity to Him through worship, goodness, and obedience. And it is said because Adam met (اجتمع) Eve (حوّاء) there, so he drew near (ازدلف) and approached her. And it is said because the sunset (المغرب) and evening (العشاء) prayers are combined (يجمع) therein.

(٤) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتُمْ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ (4) except for those of the polytheists with whom you have made a treaty, This is an exception from the polytheists (الْمُشْرِكِينَ) and a rectification (استدراك), as if it were said to them after they were commanded to renounce the covenant with the covenant-breakers (الناكثين): "But those among them with whom you made a treaty..." ثُمَّ لَمْ يَنْقُصُوكُمْ شَيْئاً (4) and who have not violated any of its terms with you, ...of the conditions of the covenant, and they did not break it, nor did they kill any of you, nor did they harm you at all. وَلَمْ يُظاهِرُوا (4) nor backed anyone against you, And they did not assist (anyone) عَلَيْكُمْ أَحَداً (4) ...of your enemies. So, فَأَتِمُّوا إِلَيْهِمْ عَهْدَهُمْ إِلى مُدَّتِهِمْ (fulfill their treaty with them until their term), meaning until the completion of their term. And do not treat the faithful (الوفيّ) like the treacherous (الغادر). إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ (4) then fulfill their treaty with them until their term. Indeed, Allah loves the Godwary. This is a justification (تعليل) and an alert that fulfilling their covenant is a matter of piety (التّقوى).

(٥) فَإِذَا انْسَلَخَ انقضى الْأَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ التي أبيح للنّاكثين أن يسيحوا فيها. (5) **فَإِذَا انْسَلَخَ الْأَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ وَخُذُوهُمْ وَاحْصُرُوهُمْ وَاقْعُدُوا لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ فَإِنْ تابُوا وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكاةَ فَخَلُّوا سَبِيلَهُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (5) When the sacred months have passed, kill the polytheists wherever you find them, and seize them, and besiege them, and lie in wait for them at every ambush. But if they repent and maintain the prayer and give the zakat, then let them go their way. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

فَإِذَا انْسَلَخَ ** (When... have passed) — meaning, elapsed — الْأَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ ** (the sacred months) — which were permitted for the covenant-breakers (الناكثين) to roam freely in them. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النحر) until ten days have passed in Rabi' al-Akhir. فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (then kill the polytheists) — meaning, the covenant-breakers (الناكثين) — حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ ** (wherever you find them) — whether in permissible territory (حلّ) or sacred territory (حرم). وَخُذُوهُمْ ** (and seize them) — meaning, and capture them. Al-Akhīdh (الأخيذ) is the captive (الأسير). وَاحْصُرُوهُمْ ** (and besiege them) — meaning, and confine them, and prevent them from reaching the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام). وَاقْعُدُوا لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ ** (and lie in wait for them at every ambush) — meaning, every passage and road by which you watch them, so that they do not spread out in the lands. فَإِنْ تابُوا ** (But if they repent) — meaning, from polytheism (الشرك) through faith (الإيمان) — وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكاةَ ** (and maintain the prayer and give the zakāt) — as confirmation of their repentance. فَخَلُّوا سَبِيلَهُمْ ** (then let them go their way) — meaning, then leave them and do not expose them to any of that [harm]. إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful) — He forgives them what has passed of their disbelief (كفرهم) and treachery (غدرهم).

(6) وَإِنْ أَحَدٌ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (6) And if any of the polytheists those whom you were commanded to confront اسْتَجارَكَ ** sought safety from you and requested your guarantee (جوارك) فَأَجِرْهُ ** so secure him حَتَّى يَسْمَعَ كَلامَ اللهِ ** and reflect upon it and become acquainted with the reality of the matter, for most of the proofs are contained within it ثُمَّ أَبْلِغْهُ مَأْمَنَهُ ** his place of security, if he does not embrace Islam. Al-Qummi said: Recite [the Quran] to him and inform him, then do not confront him until he returns to his place of safety (مأمنه). ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** what faith (الايمان) is and what the reality of that to which you invite them is. Therefore, their security (أمانهم) is necessary until they hear and reflect.

(٧) **كَيْفَ يَكُونُ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ عَهْدٌ عِنْدَ اللهِ وَعِنْدَ رَسُولِهِ ** (7) How can the polytheists have a treaty with Allah and His Apostle?

How can the polytheists have a valid treaty (عهد صحيح)? It is impossible (محال) for a treaty to be established for them while they conceal treachery (الغدر) and breach (النّكث). So, do not aspire to that. إِلَّا الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتُمْ عِنْدَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ ** (—excepting those with whom you made a treaty near the Sacred Mosque.) Meaning: But الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتُمْ ** (those with whom you made a treaty) among them عِنْدَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ ** (near the Sacred Mosque), and from whom no breach (نكث) has appeared, فَمَا اسْتَقامُوا لَكُمْ فَاسْتَقِيمُوا لَهُمْ ** (So long as they are straight with you, be straight with them.) That is, await their affair (أمرهم). If اسْتَقامُوا ** (they are straight) upon the treaty (العهد), then be straight (فَاسْتَقِيمُوا) upon fulfilling (it) (الوفاءِ). إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ.** (Indeed Allah loves the Godwary.)

(8) كَيْفَ ** (How) This is a repetition intended to deem their steadfastness upon the covenant unlikely (استبعاد). The verb is omitted because it is understood, meaning: How can there be a covenant for them?

وَإِنْ يَظْهَرُوا عَلَيْكُمْ ** (and if they prevail over you) Their condition is that if they gain victory over you, they will not observe (لَا يَرْقُبُوا فِيكُمْ), meaning: they will not regard in you...

إِلًّا ** (any kinship or covenant) ...meaning: kinship (قرابة) or alliance (حلفاً).

وَلا ذِمَّةً ** (or treaty) ...meaning: a covenant (عهداً) or a right (حقّاً).

يُرْضُونَكُمْ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ ** (They seek to please you with their mouths) ...by promising faith (الإِيمان), obedience (الطاعة), and fulfillment of the covenant (الوفا بالعهد).

وَتَأْبى قُلُوبُهُمْ ** (but their hearts refuse) ...what their mouths utter (ما يتفوّه به أفواههم).

This is a new sentence (استيناف) intended to explain their condition, which contradicts their steadfastness upon the covenant and leads to their failure to observe (rights) when they gain victory.

وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ فاسِقُونَ ** (and most of them are transgressors) ...meaning: rebellious (متمرّدون), whom no belief (عقيدة) restrains (1) and no chivalry (مروّة) deters (2). The specification of "most" (الأكثر) is due to what is found among some disbelievers (الكفّار) of abstinence (التعفف) from that which blemishes honor (يثلم العِرض) and avoidance (3) of treachery (الغدر).

(٩) اشْتَرَوْا بِآياتِ اللهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً فَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ. (They have sold Allah’s signs for a small price, and barred [people] from His way. Evil indeed is what they have been doing.)

They exchanged the Quran and its clear signs (البيّنات) ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً ** for a small worldly gain (عرضاً يسيراً), which is the following of desires (الأهواء) and lusts (الشهوات). So they averted [people] from His path (سَبِيلِهِ), meaning they deviated from it and turned others away. إِنَّهُمْ ساءَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ.**

(١٠) لا يَرْقُبُونَ فِي مُؤْمِنٍ إِلًّا وَلا ذِمَّةً وَأُولئِكَ (٤) (10) They observe toward a believer neither kinship nor covenant. And it is they who are the transgressors. They are the transgressors (الْمُعْتَدُونَ), those who exceed the limit (الغاية) in oppression (الظلم) and disbelief (الكفر). (١١) فَإِنْ تابُوا ** But if they repent, from disbelief (الكفر) and breaking the covenant (نقض العَهْدِ), وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكاةَ فَإِخْوانُكُمْ ** then they are your brethren, فِي الدِّينِ ** in faith; they have what you have, and they owe what you owe. وَنُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ And We elaborate the signs, meaning We clarify them, لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ ** for a people who know. This is an interjection (اعتراض) to urge reflection upon what has been elaborated.

footnote (١) *تزعمهم* means to restrain them (*تكفّهم*) and prevent them (*تمنعهم*). "From him (may Allah have mercy upon him)."

footnote (٢) *ردعه عنه* (He deterred him from it) is like preventing him (*منعه*), restraining him (*كفّه*), and repelling him (*وردّه*), so he was deterred (*فارتدع*).

footnote (٣) *تفادى منه* (He avoided it) means he shunned it (*تحاماه*). And the people shunned him (*تحاماه النّاس*) means they guarded against him (*توقّوه*) and avoided him (*اجتنبوا*).

footnote (٤) And the benefit (*الفائدة*) in the repetition is that the first instance is concerning the description of those who break the covenant (*العهد*), and the second is concerning the description of those who **اشْتَرَوْا بِآياتِ اللهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً** (sell the signs of Allah for a paltry price). And it is said that He only repeated it for emphasis (*تأكيداً*).

(١٢) وَإِنْ نَكَثُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ عَهْدِهِمْ وَطَعَنُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ ** (12) And if they break their oaths after their covenant, and revile your religion, and they disparage it (وعابُوه), then fight the leaders of unfaith (فَقاتِلُوا أَئِمَّةَ الْكُفْرِ). That is, "fight them." The explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (المضمر) to indicate that by doing so, they have become possessors of leadership (الرّياسة) and precedence in unfaith (الكفر), deserving of being killed (أحقّاء بالقتل). إِنَّهُمْ لا أَيْمانَ لَهُمْ ** indeed they have no oaths, in reality (على الحقيقة). Otherwise, they would not have reviled (the religion) nor broken (the covenant). It was also recited with the kasrah on the hamzah (وقرئ بكسر الهمزة).

In al-Majma', it is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means that there is no consideration (لا عبرة) for the faith (الإيمان) which they displayed. لَعَلَّهُمْ يَنْتَهُونَ ** so that they may desist. This is related to "fight" (قاتلوا). That is, let your purpose in fighting be that they desist from what they are upon, not merely inflicting harm upon them, as is the way of those who cause harm. This is due to the utmost generosity and favor of Him, the Glorified.

Al-Qummi said: This verse was revealed concerning the people of the Camel (أصحاب الجمل). The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said on the Day of the Camel (يوم الجمل): "I did not fight this covenant-breaking faction (الفئَة النّاكثة) except by a verse from the Book of Allah. Allah says: وَإِنْ نَكَثُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ (And if they break their oaths...) the verse."

In Qurb al-Isnad and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Some people from Basra came to me and asked me about Talhah and Al-Zubayr. I told them: 'They were two of the leaders of unfaith (أئمّة الكفر).' Indeed, on the Day of Basra (يوم البصرة), when Ali (peace be upon him) lined up the horses, he said to his companions: 'Do not rush against the people until I have presented an excuse (أعذر) between myself and Allah, the Exalted, and between them.' So he stood before them and said: 'O people of Basra! Do you find any injustice (جوراً) in my judgment against you?' They said: 'No.' He said: 'Or any unfairness (حيْفاً) in distribution?' They said: 'No.' He said: 'Or a desire for this world (رغبةً في دنيا) that I took for myself and my household, excluding you, so you resented me and broke my allegiance (بيعتي)?' They said: 'No.' He said: 'Did I enforce the prescribed punishments (الحدود) among you and suspend them for others?' They said: 'No.' He said: 'Then what is the matter that my allegiance is broken while the allegiance of others is not broken? I examined the matter thoroughly (ضربت الأمر أنفه وعينه), and I found nothing but unfaith (الكفر) or the sword.' Then he turned to his companions and said: 'Indeed, Allah, the Exalted, says in His Book: وَإِنْ نَكَثُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ (And if they break their oaths...) the verse."

Then Ali (peace be upon him) said: "By Him Who split the seed and created the soul, and chose Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم for prophethood, they are indeed the people referred to in this verse, and they have not been fought since it was revealed."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever reviles this religion of yours has committed unfaith (كفر). Allah said: وَطَعَنُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ فَقاتِلُوا أَئِمَّةَ الْكُفْرِ إِنَّهُمْ لا أَيْمانَ لَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَنْتَهُونَ. (and revile your religion, then fight the leaders of unfaith—indeed they have no oaths—so that they may desist.)"

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Allah has excused me regarding Talhah and Al-Zubayr. They pledged allegiance to me willingly, not under coercion, then they broke my allegiance without any new event (حدث) having occurred on my part. By Allah, the people referred to in this verse have not been fought since it was revealed until I fought them: وَإِنْ نَكَثُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ (And if they break their oaths...) the verse." And there are many narrations with this meaning.

(١٣) أَلا تُقاتِلُونَ قَوْماً ** (This is) an incitement to fight. نَكَثُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ ** which they swore with the Messenger صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers, that they would not assist against them, yet they did assist. وَهَمُّوا بِإِخْراجِ الرَّسُولِ ** when they consulted about his affair in Dar al-Nadwah (بدار النّدوة), until Allah permitted him to emigrate (الهجرة), so he departed himself, as was previously mentioned in His saying: وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَهُمْ بَدَؤُكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** with enmity and fighting, and the aggressor is more unjust (الْبَادِئُ أَظْلَمُ). So what prevents you from fighting them in kind? أَتَخْشَوْنَهُمْ ** (١) Do you abandon fighting them out of fear that some harm may befall you from them? فَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشَوْهُ ** so fight His enemies and do not abandon His command. **إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** For the believer fears none but his Lord.

أَلا تُقاتِلُونَ قَوْماً نَكَثُوا أَيْمانَهُمْ وَهَمُّوا بِإِخْراجِ الرَّسُولِ وَهُمْ بَدَؤُكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ أَتَخْشَوْنَهُمْ فَاللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشَوْهُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (13) (13) Are you not going to fight a people who broke their oaths and resolved to expel the Messenger, and they attacked you first? Do you fear them? Allah is worthier of your fear, if you are faithful.

(١٤) قاتِلُوهُمْ ** (14) Fight them. This is a command (أمر) to fight after clarifying the necessity (موجبه) for it and the reproach (التّوبيخ) for abandoning it. يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللهُ بِأَيْدِيكُمْ وَيُخْزِهِمْ وَيَنْصُرْكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** Allah will punish them by your hands and disgrace them, and help you against them, This is a promise (وَعْدٌ) to them that if they fight them, He will grant them victory (النّصر) over them and enable them to kill them and humiliate them (وإذلالهم). وَيَشْفِ صُدُورَ قَوْمٍ مُؤْمِنِينَ. and heal the breasts of a faithful folk.

(١٥) وَيُذْهِبْ غَيْظَ قُلُوبِهِمْ (15. and remove the rage of their hearts.) due to the harm (المكروه) they suffered from them. And Allah has fulfilled all these promises, and the Ayah is among the proofs (دلائل) of Prophethood (النّبوّة).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Abu al-A'azz al-Taymi: He said: I was standing between two ranks when I looked at al-Abbas ibn Rabi'ah ibn al-Harith ibn Abd al-Muttalib, who was clad in armor (شاك في السلاح), when a caller (هاتف) from the people of Syria (أهل الشّام) called out to him—he was called Arar ibn Adham—"O Abbas, come forth to single combat (البراز)!" Then they fought face-to-face (تكافحا) (2) with their swords for a long time (مليّاً) (3), neither of them reaching the other due to the completeness of his armor (لامته), until al-Abbas struck down the Syrian's shield (درع الشّاميّ). He then lunged at him with the sword, piercing the Syrian's ribs (جوانح الشاميّ). The Syrian fell dead (صريعاً), and the people shouted takbir (تكبيرة) so loudly that the earth shook because of it. Then I heard a speaker saying: قاتِلُوهُمْ يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللهُ بِأَيْدِيكُمْ (Fight them! Allah will punish them by your hands) and the rest of the Ayah. I turned around, and behold, it was the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) (peace be upon him).

وَيَتُوبُ اللهُ عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ (and Allah turns clemently to whomever He wishes.) This is a new statement (استيناف أخبار) that some of them will repent from their disbelief (كفره), and that has also occurred. وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ (and Allah is all-knowing,) of what was and what will be. حَكِيمٌ (all-wise.) He does nothing except that which contains wisdom (الحكمة).

(١٦) أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَنْ تُتْرَكُوا ** (16) (Do you reckon that you will be let alone) Am (أم) is disconnected (منقطعة), and the interrogative particle (الهمزة) carries the meaning of reproach (التوبيخ). This means that you will not be left upon the state you are in. وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللهُ الَّذِينَ جاهَدُوا مِنْكُمْ ** (while Allah has not yet ascertained those of you who wage jihad) And the sincere ones (المخلصون) among you have not yet been distinguished—and they are those who wage jihad in the way of Allah for the sake of Allah's Countenance (لوجه الله). وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلا رَسُولِهِ وَلَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلِيجَةً ** (and do not take any confidant besides Allah and His Apostle and the faithful?) Meaning the sincere ones (المخلصين) are those who do not take an inner circle (بطانة) besides them (Allah, His Messenger, and the believers) whom they befriend (يوالونهم) and to whom they reveal their secrets (ويفشون إليهم أسرارهم). Since lammā (لمّا) indicates that something is expected, it was said that by negating the knowledge (نفي العلم), He intended the negation of the known/manifested reality (نفي المعلوم). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, He has not yet seen (لمّا يرى), so He established knowledge (العلم) in place of sight (الرّؤية), because He had already known before they acted. And from al-Baqir (عليه السلام): He means by the believers (المؤمنين) the Household of Muhammad (صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم), and al-walijah (الوليجة) is the inner circle (البطانة). In al-Kafi, narrated from him (عليه السلام): He means by the believers (المؤمنين) the Imams (عليهم السلام). And from him (عليه السلام): "Do not take a walijah (وليجة) besides Allah, lest you cease to be believers (مؤمنين). For every connection (سبب), lineage (نسب), kinship (قرابة), inner circle (وليجة), innovation (بدعة), and ambiguity (شبهة) is severed, except what the Quran has affirmed." And from Abu Muhammad al-Zaki (عليه السلام): Al-walijah (الوليجة) is the one who is set up besides the rightful guardian (وليّ الأمر). And the believers (المؤمنون) in this place are the Imams (عليهم السلام) who grant security on behalf of Allah (يؤمنون على الله), so He permits their security (فيجيز أمانهم). وَاللهُ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (and Allah is well aware of what you do.) He knows your purpose (غرضكم) in this.

(١٧) ما كانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (It is not for the polytheists) It was not right for them, nor was it proper, أَنْ يَعْمُرُوا مَساجِدَ اللهِ ** (to maintain the mosques of Allah) any of the mosques, let alone the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام). And it was read in the singular (بالتّوحيد). شاهِدِينَ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ بِالْكُفْرِ (while bearing witness to their own faithlessness) by displaying polytheism (الشرك) and setting up idols (الأصنام) around the House (البيت).

In al-Jawami', it is narrated that the Muslims reproached the prisoners of Badr and rebuked al-Abbas for fighting the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and cutting off kinship (قطيعة الرّحم). So al-Abbas said: "You mention our faults and conceal our virtues (محاسننا)." They asked: "Do you have virtues?" He said: "Yes, we maintain the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), and we guard (نحجب) the Ka'bah, and we provide water for the pilgrims (الحجيج), and we free the captive (العاني) (1)." So [the Ayah] was revealed: أُولئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ (They are the ones whose works have failed) which are the maintenance (العمارة), the provision of water (السّقاية), the guardianship (الحجابة), and the freeing of captives (فكّ العناة) by which they boast, due to the polytheism (الشرك) that accompanied them. **وَفِي النَّارِ هُمْ خالِدُونَ ** (and they shall remain in the Fire forever) because of it.

(١٨) إِنَّما يَعْمُرُ مَساجِدَ اللهِ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَأَقامَ الصَّلاةَ وَآتَى الزَّكاةَ (١٨) (18) The mosques of Allah shall be built only by those who have faith in Allah and the Last Day, and establish the prayer, and give the zakat,

Indeed, the maintenance (عمارتها) of them is proper only for these people who combine the intellectual (العلميّة) and practical (العمليّة) perfections. And the maintenance (العمارة) includes their construction (بناؤها), the repair (رمّ) of whatever has become dilapidated (استرمّ) from them, sweeping them, cleaning them, illuminating them with lamps (بالسّرج), visiting them for worship (للعبادة), remembrance (الذّكر), studying knowledge (درس العلم), and protecting them from that for which they were not built, such as worldly talk (حديث الدنيا).

And in the Hadith Qudsi: "Indeed, My houses on Earth are the mosques, and My visitors therein are those who maintain them (عمّارها). So, blessedness (طوبى) is for a servant who purifies himself in his house, then visits Me in My house. It is incumbent upon the Visited One (Allah) to honor His visitor."

And in the Prophetic Hadith صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "In the latter days, people from My Ummah will come who frequent the mosques and sit therein in circles (حلقاً). Their remembrance (ذكرهم) is the world and the love of the world. Do not sit with them, for Allah has no need of them."

**وَلَمْ يَخْشَ إِلَّا اللهَ ** (18) and fear no one except Allah.

Meaning: in matters of religion (في أبواب الدين), by not preferring the pleasure (رضاء) of anyone else over the pleasure (رضاء) of Allah. For indeed, fear (الخشية) of dangers (المحاذير) is innate (جبليّة), and the sensible person can scarcely restrain himself from it.

**فَعَسى أُولئِكَ أَنْ يَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُهْتَدِينَ ** (18) Maybe such will be among the guided.

He mentioned this using the form of expectation (صيغة التوقع) in order to cut off the hopes (أطماع) of the polytheists (المشركين) for guidance (الاهتداء) and for their deeds to be beneficial (الانتفاع بأعمالهم).

أَجَعَلْتُمْ سِقايَةَ الْحاجِّ وَعِمارَةَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ كَمَنْ آمَنَ ** (Do you regard the provision of water to the pilgrims and the maintenance of the Holy Mosque as being on a par with someone who has faith) like the faith of one who has faith بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَجاهَدَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (in Allah and the Last Day and waged Jihad in the way of Allah)? Or (do you mean) have you made the people of providing water (السقاية) and maintenance (العمارة) like one who has faith?

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that He read (سقاة الحاجّ) (the providers of water to the pilgrims) and (عمرة المسجد الحرام) (the maintenance of the Holy Mosque).

Al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): This Ayah was revealed concerning Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), His saying: كَمَنْ آمَنَ بِاللهِ (as someone who has faith in Allah) [to the end of] the Ayah.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Ali (peace be upon him), al-Abbas, and Shaybah. Al-Abbas said: "I am superior because the provision of water to the pilgrims (سقاية الحاجّ) is in my hand." And Shaybah said: "I am superior because the guardianship of the House (حجابة البيت) is in my hand." And Ali said: "I am superior, for I had faith before both of you, then I emigrated (هاجرت) and waged Jihad (وجاهدت)." So they were satisfied with the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and Allah revealed (the Ayah).

And in al-Majma' is what is close to this, and it added: "I struck your snouts (خرطومكما) (1) with the sword until you both had faith in Allah."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) what is similar in meaning, and he mentioned Uthman ibn Abi Shaybah instead of Shaybah.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): It was revealed concerning Hamzah, Ali, Ja'far, al-Abbas, and Shaybah, that they boasted about the provision of water (السّقاية) and the guardianship (الحجابة), so Allah revealed (the Ayah). And Ali, Hamzah, and Ja'far were those who had faith بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَجاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ (in Allah and the Last Day and waged Jihad in the way of) (2) Allah.

لا يَسْتَوُونَ عِنْدَ اللهِ وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (They are not equal in Allah’s sight, and Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot.) due to polytheism (بالشّرك) and those who equate them (the polytheists) with the believers.

(1) Al-Ani (العاني) is the captive (الأسير). From this (comes the saying): "Feed the hungry and release the captive (العاني)." And every person who is humbled (ذلّ), submissive (استكان), and obedient (خضع) has become ‘ana (عنى), and he is ‘an (عان). The woman is ‘aniyah (عانية), and the plural is ‘awan (عوان). From this is the narration: "Fear Allah regarding women, for they are ‘awan (عوان) with you," meaning captives (أسراء) like prisoners.

(2) Ramamtu (رممت) the thing, arummuhu (ارُمُّه) and arimahu (ارِمَه), ramman (رمّاً) and marammah (مرمّة), when you repaired (أصلحته) it.

(20) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهاجَرُوا وَجاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ أَعْظَمُ دَرَجَةً عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (Those who have believed and emigrated and struggled in the way of Allah with their possessions and their lives have a greater rank with Allah) [They are] of greater rank (درجة) and possess more honor (كرامة) than those who have not combined these attributes. وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفائِزُونَ (and it is such who are the triumphant) They are the ones distinguished by triumph (الفوز) and the attainment of the best reward (الحسنى) with Allah.

(٢١) يُبَشِّرُهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنْهُ وَرِضْوانٍ وَجَنَّاتٍ لَهُمْ فِيها نَعِيمٌ مُقِيمٌ ** (21) Their Lord gives them the good news of a mercy from Him, and [His] pleasure, and gardens where they will have lasting bliss. [The meaning of مُقِيمٌ is] lasting (دائمٌ). And the indefiniteness (التنكير) of that which is given as good news is an intimation that it is beyond description (التوصيف) and definition (التعريف). (٢٢) خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً إِنَّ اللهَ عِنْدَهُ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ ** (22) They will abide in them forever. Indeed, with Allah there is a great reward. Every reward is deemed insignificant compared to it.

footnote (١) قوله تعالى **سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ **هو بضمّ الخاء والأنف وهو أكرم موضع في الوجه كما أنّ الوجه أكرم موضع في الجسد (1) The Almighty's saying: سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الْخُرْطُومِ ** (We will brand him on the snout) [The word] الْخُرْطُومِ is with a ḍammah on the khā' (بضمّ الخاء) and it means the nose (الأنف). It is the noblest place on the face, just as the face is the noblest place on the body.

footnote (٢) وروي انّ المشركين قالوا لليهود نحنُ سقات الحجيج وعمّار المسجد الحرام فنحن أفضل أم محمّد وأصحابه فقالت اليهود أنتم أفضل فنزلت. (2) It is narrated that the polytheists (المشركين) said to the Jews (اليهود): "We are the providers of drink to the pilgrims (سقات الحجيج) and the maintainers of the Sacred Mosque (عمّار المسجد الحرام). Are we better, or Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his companions?" The Jews replied: "You are better." So, [this Ayah] was revealed.

(٢٣) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّخِذُوا آباءَكُمْ وَإِخْوانَكُمْ أَوْلِياءَ إِنِ اسْتَحَبُّوا الْكُفْرَ عَلَى الْإِيمانِ ** (23) O you who have faith! Do not take your fathers and brothers for guardians if they prefer disbelief to faith. meaning, they chose it over it. It is said that when they were commanded to emigrate (الهجرة), their relatives prevented them from it, and some of them would abandon it for their sake, so this Ayah was revealed.

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It was revealed concerning Hatib ibn Abi Balta'ah, when he wrote to Quraysh informing them of the news of the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم when He intended the conquest of Mecca (مكّة).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The disbelief (الكفر) referred to inwardly (في الباطن) in this Ayah is the guardianship (ولاية) of the First and the Second, and the faith (الإِيمان) is the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him).

وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ مِنْكُمْ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** And whoever of you takes them for guardians—it is they who are the wrongdoers. by placing the loyalty (الموالاة) in other than its rightful place.

(٢٤) قُلْ إِنْ كانَ آباؤُكُمْ وَأَبْناؤُكُمْ وَإِخْوانُكُمْ وَأَزْواجُكُمْ وَعَشِيرَتُكُمْ وَأَمْوالٌ اقْتَرَفْتُمُوها وَتِجارَةٌ تَخْشَوْنَ كَسادَها وَمَساكِنُ تَرْضَوْنَها أَحَبَّ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَجِهادٍ فِي سَبِيلِهِ فَتَرَبَّصُوا حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ اللهُ بِأَمْرِهِ وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ (Say, 'If your fathers, and your sons, and your brothers, and your wives, and your kinsfolk, and the possessions you have acquired, and the trade you fear may decline, and the dwellings you are fond of, are dearer to you than Allah and His Apostle and jihad in His way, then wait until Allah issues His command, and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot.')

The Almighty's saying: (٢٤) قُلْ إِنْ كانَ آباؤُكُمْ وَأَبْناؤُكُمْ وَإِخْوانُكُمْ وَأَزْواجُكُمْ وَعَشِيرَتُكُمْ ** meaning your relatives (أقرباؤكم). And it was read (عشيراتكم). وَأَمْوالٌ اقْتَرَفْتُمُوها ** meaning you have earned them (اكتسبتموها). وَتِجارَةٌ تَخْشَوْنَ كَسادَها وَمَساكِنُ تَرْضَوْنَها أَحَبَّ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَجِهادٍ فِي سَبِيلِهِ فَتَرَبَّصُوا (١) حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ اللهُ بِأَمْرِهِ ** meaning His threat (وعيده), and the command (الأمر) is a punishment (عقوبة). وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ meaning He does not guide them (لا يرشدهم).

Al-Qummi said: When the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) announced in Mecca that no polytheist (مشرك) should enter the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) after that year, Quraysh became severely distressed. They said: "Our trade is gone, our families are lost, and our homes are ruined." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning that: قُلْ ** (Say), O Muhammad, إِنْ كانَ آباؤُكُمْ ** (If your fathers...)—the rest of the verse.

I say: There is a great severity (تشديد عظيم) in this verse, and few are those who escape it. And in the Hadith: None of you will find the taste of faith (طعم الإِيمان) until he loves for the sake of Allah and hates for the sake of Allah.

(٢٥) لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللهُ فِي مَواطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ (٢) (Allah has certainly helped you in many situations) meaning the situations (مواطن) of war, which are its locations (مواقعها) and its occurrences (موافقها). In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Hadi (peace be upon him): that they were eighty situations (موطناً). وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ ** (and on the day of Hunayn,) which is a valley between Mecca (مكّة) and Ta'if (طائف). إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ ** (when your great number impressed you,) In al-Jawami': When they met, a man from the Muslims said, "We will not be defeated today due to fewness (قلّة)." His saying displeased the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. It was said that the one who said it was Abu Bakr. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding His saying: إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ ** (when your great number impressed you,) up to His saying: ثُمَّ وَلَّيْتُمْ مُدْبِرِينَ ** (then you turned your backs in retreat,) He said: "Abu so-and-so (أبو فلان)." فَلَمْ تُغْنِ عَنْكُمْ ** (but it did not avail you) the great number (الكثرة) شَيْئاً ** (in any way,) of sufficiency (الغنى) or protection from the enemy's commands (أوامر العدوّ). That was because the word of self-admiration (كلمة الإعجاب) overtook them. وَضاقَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَرْضُ بِما رَحُبَتْ ** (and the earth became narrow for you with all its breadth,) meaning with its vastness (بسعتها). You could not find therein a resting place (مقرّاً) to which your souls could be reassured, due to the intensity of the terror (الرّعب). ثُمَّ وَلَّيْتُمْ مُدْبِرِينَ ** (then you turned your backs in retreat,) meaning, fleeing (منهزمين).

footnote (١) I waited for the matter, a waiting (*تربّصاً*), meaning I awaited it (*انتظرته*). And I waited for the matter concerning so-and-so (*وتربّصت بفلان الأمر*), meaning I expected its descent upon him (*توقّعت نزوله به*).

footnote (٢) In *al-Kafi*, narrated from Ali, from some of his companions who mentioned it: He said: When al-Mutawakkil was poisoned, he vowed that if he recovered, he would give a large amount of wealth (*مال كثير*) in charity. When he recovered, he asked the jurists (*الفقهاء*) about the limit of a large amount of wealth, and they differed regarding it. Some said one hundred thousand, and others said ten thousand, and they offered various sayings concerning it, so the matter became confusing for him. Then a man from his companions (*ندمائه*), named Saf'an (*صفعان*), said: "Should you send for this black man (*الأسود*) and ask him about it?" Al-Mutawakkil said to him: "Who do you mean, woe betide you?" He said: "The son of..."

(٢٦) ثُمَّ أَنْزَلَ اللهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ وَعَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَنْزَلَ جُنُوداً لَمْ تَرَوْها وَعَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا. (Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Apostle and upon the faithful, and sent down hosts that you did not see, and He punished the faithless.) Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is the killing, meaning the punishment (العذاب). وَذلِكَ جَزاءُ الْكافِرِينَ ** (And that is the requital of the faithless.) Al-Qummi: The reason for the Battle of Hunayn was that when the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم set out for the conquest of Mecca, He made it appear that He intended (to fight) Hawazin. The news reached Hawazin, so they prepared, gathered forces and weapons, and the chiefs of Hawazin assembled under Malik ibn Awf al-Nadri, whom they made their leader. They set out, driving their wealth, women, and children with them, and proceeded until they encamped at Awtas. He said: When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم was informed of the gathering of Hawazin at Awtas, He gathered the tribes, encouraged them in Jihad (الجهاد), promised them victory, and (informed them) that Allah had promised Him that He would grant Him their wealth, women, and children as spoils. So the people were eager and marched under their banners. He tied the great standard (اللّواء الأكبر) and handed it to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). He commanded everyone who entered Mecca with a banner to carry it. He marched out with twelve thousand men, ten thousand of whom were those who had been with Him.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: There were with Him a thousand men from Banu Sulaym, whose chief was Abbas ibn Mirdas al-Sulami, and a thousand men from Muzaynah (1). He said: They proceeded until they were a distance of part of a night's journey from the enemy. He said: And Malik ibn Awf said to his people: "Let every man among you place his family or his wealth behind his back, break the sheaths of your swords, and lie in ambush in the ravines of this valley and among the trees. When it is the darkness (غَلَس) of dawn, charge with the charge of one man and crush (اهدّوا) the people, for Muhammad has not met anyone who is skilled in warfare." He said: When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed the dawn prayer (الغداة), He descended into the Valley of Hunayn, which was a valley with a steep descent. Banu Sulaym were at the vanguard, and the battalions of Hawazin emerged against them from every direction. Banu Sulaym were routed, and those behind them were routed, and no one remained except that he fled.

And the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) remained fighting them with a small group of people. The routed ones passed by the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, not turning (3) towards anything. Al-Abbas was holding the bridle of the Messenger of Allah's mule صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم on His right, and Abu Sufyan ibn al-Harith ibn Abd al-Muttalib was on His left. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم approached, calling out: "O company of the Ansar (الأنصار), where are you going? I am the Messenger of Allah!" But no one turned towards Him. And Nusaybah bint Ka'b al-Maziniyyah was throwing (تحثو), meaning casting, dust into the faces of the routed ones, saying: "Where are you fleeing to, away from Allah and His Messenger?"

And Umar passed by her, so she said: "Woe upon you! What is this that you have done?" He replied to her: "This is the decree of Allah." When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم saw the rout, He spurred (4) His mule towards Ali, having drawn His sword. He said: "O Abbas"—and he was a man with a loud, high voice—"Ascend this small hill (5) (الظرب) and call out: 'O Companions of the Cow (البقرة)! O Companions of the Tree (الشّجرة)! Where are you fleeing? This is the Messenger of Allah!'" Then the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم raised His hand and said: "O Allah, to You belongs all praise, and to You is the complaint, and You are the One Whose help is sought (المستعان)." Then Jibril (peace be upon him) descended upon Him and said: "O Messenger of Allah, You have prayed with the prayer that Musa (peace be upon him) prayed when Allah split the sea and saved him from Pharaoh." Then the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Abu Sufyan ibn al-Harith: "Hand me a handful of pebbles." So he handed them to Him, and He threw them into the faces of the polytheists, then said: "May the faces be deformed (شاهت الوجوه)." Then He raised His head to the sky and said: "O Allah, if You destroy this small band (العصابة), You will not be worshipped; and if You wish that You not be worshipped, You will not be worshipped."

When the Ansar (الأنصار) heard the call of al-Abbas, they turned back and broke the sheaths of their swords, saying: "At Your service (لبّيك)!" They passed by the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, feeling ashamed to return to Him, and joined the standard (الرّاية). The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to al-Abbas: "Who are these, O Abu al-Fadl?" He replied: "O Messenger of Allah, these are the Ansar." The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Now the battle has intensified (1) (حمي الوَطيس)," and the victory descended from Allah. Hawazin was routed, and they (the Muslims) were hearing the clanking of weapons in the air. They fled in every direction, and Allah and His Messenger gained their wealth, women, and children as spoils. This is the saying of Allah: لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللهُ فِي مَواطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ. (Certainly Allah has helped you in many situations, and on the day of Hunayn.)

He said: And a man from Banu Nasr ibn Mu'awiyah, named Shajarah ibn Rabi'ah, who was a captive in their hands, said to the believers: "Where are the piebald horses (2) (الخيل البلق) and the men wearing white garments? Indeed, our killing was by their hands, and we did not see you among them except like a mole (الشّامة)." They said: "Those were the angels (الملائكة)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked what al-Sakinah (السّكينة) is. He replied: "A wind from Paradise (الجنّة) that has a face like the face of a human, with a fragrance sweeter than musk (المسك). It is that which Allah sent down upon His Messenger at Hunayn, and He routed the polytheists."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) killed forty (men) **يَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ ** (on the day of Hunayn).

Al-Rida (peace be upon him) said to him, while he was skilled in something of this nature: "O Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), if I extract you from this, then I shall have such-and-such from you, otherwise, strike me with one hundred lashes (مقرعة)." Al-Mutawakkil said: "I am satisfied, O Ja'far ibn Mahmud. Go to him and ask him about the limit (حدّ) of abundant wealth (المال الكثير)." So Ja'far ibn Mahmud went to Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad (peace be upon them both) and asked him about the limit of abundant wealth. He said: "Abundant is eighty." Ja'far said to him: "O my Master (يا سيّدي), I believe he will ask me about the reason (العلّة) for it." Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) said, meaning: "The proof (الدّليل) for it is His saying, the Almighty: فِي مَواطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ (in many situations). We counted those situations, and they were eighty."

(١) Muzaynah (مزينة) is a tribe from Mudar (مضر).

(٢) Al-Ghalas (الغَلَس) (with vowelization) is the darkness at the end of the night.

(٣) Meaning: No one stopped for anyone else, nor waited for him.

(٤) One spurs (ركضت) the animal when one strikes it with his leg, urging it on.

(٥) Al-Dharib (الظرب) (like katif (ككتف)) is that which protrudes from the stones and whose edge is sharp, or the small, flat mountain. Plural: Dhirab (ظراب).

(٢٧) ثُمَّ يَتُوبُ اللهُ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (27) Then Allah will relent after that toward whomever He wishes. from among them, by granting them success (التّوفيق) to embrace Islam. And Allah is غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (all-forgiving, all-merciful); He overlooks their faults (يتجاوز عنهم) and bestows His favor upon them (ويتفضّل عليهم).

It was narrated: That some people from among them came to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآلِهِ وسلم and embraced Islam. They said: "O Messenger of Allah, you are the best of people and the most righteous of them. Our families and children have been taken captive, and our wealth has been seized. On that day, six thousand souls were taken captive, and camels and sheep were taken in countless numbers."

He said: "Choose either your captives (سباياكم) or your wealth (أموالكم)."

They said: "We would not equate lineage (الأحساب) with anything."

The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم then spoke, saying: "These people have come as Muslims, and we gave them the choice between the children (الذّراري) and the wealth, but they did not equate lineage (الأحساب) with anything. So, whoever holds a captive and willingly returns him, that is his concern (فشأنه). And whoever does not, let him give him to us, and it shall be a loan (قرضاً) upon us until we acquire something, then we shall give him its replacement (مكانه)."

They said: "We are satisfied (رضينا) and submit (سلّمنا)."

He said: "I do not know; perhaps there is someone among you who is not satisfied. So, command your chiefs (عرفاءكم) to report back to us."

They reported back that they were indeed satisfied.

(28) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّمَا الْمُشْرِكُونَ نَجَسٌ ** (O you who have faith! The polytheists are indeed unclean.) due to the foulness of their inner being (باطنهم). فَلا يَقْرَبُوا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرامَ بَعْدَ عامِهِمْ هذا وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ عَيْلَةً ** (so let them not approach the Holy Mosque after this year of theirs. And if you fear poverty,) meaning poverty (فقراً) due to their being prevented from the Sanctuary (الحرم) and the cutting off of the gains (المكاسب) and benefits (المنافع) that came to you from their arrival. فَسَوْفَ يُغْنِيكُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (Allah will enrich you out of His grace,) from His giving (عطائه) and His favor (تفضّله) in another manner, if He wills. It was said (قيل): He restricted it with the Will (بالمشيئة) so that hopes (الآمال) would be cut off (from reliance on anything other than) Allah, the Exalted, and His Prophet, based on the fact that He is the One Who bestows favor (متفضّل) in that, and that the promised wealth (الغنى الموعود) would be for some and not others, and in some years and not others. And He fulfilled His promise by sending down the sky upon them abundantly (مدراراً), and He granted success (وفّق) to a group of the people of Yemen to embrace Islam, so they carried food to Mecca. Then He granted them the conquest of lands (البلاد) and spoils (الغنائم), and people turned towards them from the corners of the earth (أقطار الأرض). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ ** (if He wishes. Indeed, Allah is all-knowing,) of your conditions (أحوالكم). **حَكِيمٌ ** (all-wise.) in what He gives and what He withholds.

(29) قاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَلا بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ** (29) (Fight those who do not believe in Allah, nor in the Last Day) meaning, they do not believe in both of them in the manner that is proper, for their belief is as if it were no belief. وَلا يُحَرِّمُونَ ما حَرَّمَ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ ** (nor hold as forbidden what Allah and His Apostle have forbidden) that which is established as forbidden by the Book (الكتاب) and the Sunnah (السنة), وَلا يَدِينُونَ دِينَ الْحَقِ (nor profess the religion of truth) the established truth which abrogates (ناسخ) all other religions and invalidates them مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ ** (from among those who were given the Book). This is a clarification for الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (those who do not believe) حَتَّى يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ ** (until they pay the tribute), that which is determined for them to give, from the recompense (جزي) of their religion when He decrees it عَنْ يَدٍ ** (out of hand), willingly, not refusing وَهُمْ صاغِرُونَ ** (and are abased), humbled, meaning it is taken from them while they are in a state of humiliation (الصّغار) and abasement (الذّلّ).

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah sent Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم with five swords. Until he said: And the second sword is against the People of the Covenant (أهل الذمّة). Allah the Almighty said: وَقُولُوا لِلنَّاسِ حُسْناً (and speak kindly to people). This verse was revealed concerning the People of the Covenant (أهل الذمّة), then it was abrogated (نسخها) by His saying, the Glorified: **قاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَلا بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ** (Fight those who do not believe in Allah, nor in the Last Day) the verse.

So, whoever among them is in the Abode of Islam (دار الإسلام), nothing is accepted from them except the tribute (الجزية) or death (القتل). They have no spoils (فيء), and their offspring are captives (سبي). If they accept the tribute (الجزية) for themselves, their captivity (سبي) becomes forbidden for us, their wealth becomes forbidden, and marriage with their women (مناكحهم) becomes lawful for us. And whoever among them is in the Abode of War (دار الحرب), their captivity (سبي) and their wealth become lawful for us, but marriage with their women (مناكحتهم) is not lawful for us. Nothing is accepted from them except entering the Abode of Islam (دار الإسلام), or the tribute (الجزية), or death (القتل).

And al-Ayyashi narrated that which is close to it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Magians (المجوس): Did they have a prophet? He said: "Yes. Has the letter of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم to the people of Mecca (أهل مكّة) not reached you, [saying] 'Embrace Islam, or else be warned of war'?" So they wrote to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم [saying]: "Take the tribute (الجزية) from us and leave us to the worship of idols (عبادة الأوثان)." The Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم wrote back to them: "I do not take the tribute (الجزية) except from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب)." So they wrote to Him, intending thereby to accuse Him of lying: "You claimed that you do not take the tribute (الجزية) except from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), yet you took the tribute (الجزية) from the Magians (مجوس) of Hajar (1)." The Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم wrote back to them: "Indeed, the Magians (المجوس) had a prophet whom they killed, and a book which they burned. Their prophet brought them their book in twelve thousand ox hides."

And in it (al-Kafi), and in al-Faqih and al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about women, how the tribute (الجزية) was dropped and lifted from them. He said: "Because the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم forbade the killing of women and children in the Abode of War (دار الحرب), unless they fight. And even if she fights, refrain from [killing] her as long as you can, and you do not fear a breach [in defense]. Since He forbade their killing in the Abode of War (دار الحرب), this was even more appropriate in the Abode of Islam (دار الإِسلام). If she refused to pay the tribute (الجزية), it would not be possible to kill her. Since it was not possible to kill her, the tribute (الجزية) was lifted from her. If the men refused and declined to pay the tribute (الجزية), they would be violators of the covenant (ناقضين للعهد), and their blood and killing would become lawful, because the killing of men is permissible in the Abode of Polytheism (دار الشّرك). Likewise, the crippled (المقعدة) among the people of polytheism (أهل الشّرك) and the covenant (الذمّة), the blind, the decrepit old man (الشيخ الفاني), the woman, and the children in the land of war (أرض الحرب). And for this reason, the tribute (الجزية) was lifted from them."

And in al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The Sunnah (السّنة) has established that the tribute (الجزية) is not taken from the mentally deficient (المعتوه) (1) nor from the one whose intellect is overcome (المغلُوب على عقله).

And in both of them (al-Kafi and al-Faqih), and in al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked: "What is the limit (حدّ) of the tribute (الجزية) upon the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب)? Is there a fixed amount (شيء موظف) upon them in this regard that they should not exceed to anything else?" He said: "That is up to the Imam (الإِمام). He takes from every person among them whatever He wills, according to what he possesses and what he can bear. They are merely a people who ransomed themselves from being enslaved or killed. So, the tribute (الجزية) is taken from them according to what they can bear. He has the right to take it from them until they embrace Islam, for Allah the Almighty said: حَتَّى يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ يَدٍ وَهُمْ صاغِرُونَ ** (until they pay the tribute out of hand and are abased). And how can he be abased (صاغراً) if he does not care (2) about what is taken from him? No, [it must be taken] until he feels humiliation (ذلًّا) for what was taken from him, so that he suffers pain because of it, and thus embraces Islam."

And in both of them (al-Kafi and al-Faqih), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), concerning the People of the Tribute (أهل الجزية): "Is anything taken from their wealth and livestock besides the tribute (الجزية)?" He said: "No."

footnote (1). Hajar (*هَجَر*) (with vowel marks) is a town in Yemen, or a name for the entire land of Bahrain, or a village that was near Medina.

(٣٠) وَقالَتِ الْيَهُودُ عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ ** (30) And the Jews say, 'Ezra is the son of Allah.' Only some of them said that, and not all of them said it. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: He demanded proof (الحجة) from them, so they said: "Because he revived the Torah for the Children of Israel after it had vanished, and no one would do this except if he were His son." So the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "How did عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ ** (Ezra become the son of Allah) instead of Moses, who brought them the Torah and from whom they saw the miracles that you know? If Ezra was the son of Allah due to the honor shown to him by reviving the Torah, then Moses was certainly more deserving and worthier of prophethood (بالنبوّة أحق وأولى)." (End of the narrative الحديث). وَقالَتِ النَّصارى الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ اللهِ (And the Christians say, 'The Messiah is the son of Allah.') This is also the saying of some of them. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: He demanded proof (الحجة) from them, so they said: "When Allah manifested through the hand of Jesus (peace be upon him) the wondrous things He manifested, He surely took him as a son by way of honor (على جهة الكرامة)." So the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "You have already heard what I said to the Jews concerning this meaning that you have mentioned." Then He repeated all of that, and they fell silent. (End of the narrative الحديث). ذلِكَ قَوْلُهُمْ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ (That is an utterance of their mouths). They invented it with their mouths; no scripture came to them with it, and they have no proof (حجة) for it. يُضاهِؤُنَ قَوْلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (They imitate the saying of the faithless). Their saying imitates قَوْلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (the saying of those who were faithless before), such as those who claimed that the angels are the daughters of Allah. قاتَلَهُمُ اللهُ (May Allah assail them!). In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a narrative: Meaning, may Allah curse them (لعنهم الله), so He named the curse (اللعنة) "assailing" (قتالاً). أَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ (How are they being led astray?) How are they turned away from the truth (الحقّ)? In al-Majalis and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "The wrath of Allah intensified upon the Jews when they said عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ ** (Ezra is the son of Allah), and the wrath of Allah intensified upon the Christians when they said الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ اللهِ ** (The Messiah is the son of Allah), and the wrath of Allah intensified upon whoever spilled my blood and harmed me concerning my progeny (عترتي)."

(٣١) اتَّخَذُوا أَحْبارَهُمْ وَرُهْبانَهُمْ أَرْباباً مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (31) They have taken their scribes and monks as lords besides Allah, by obeying them in prohibiting what Allah has made lawful and making lawful what Allah has made unlawful. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, they did not call them to the worship of themselves, and had they called them to the worship of themselves, they would not have responded to them. Rather, they made unlawful things lawful for them and made lawful things unlawful for them, so they worshipped them without realizing it." And there are many narratives (أخبار) with this meaning. وَالْمَسِيحَ ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ ** and the Messiah, son of Mary. by elevating him to worship. Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "As for the Messiah (المسيح), they disobeyed him and magnified him in their souls until they claimed that he was a god and that he was the son of Allah. A group among them said he was the third of three, and a group among them said he was Allah. As for their scribes (أَحْبارَهُمْ) and their monks (وَرُهْبانَهُمْ), they obeyed them, accepted their sayings, followed what they commanded them, and submitted to what they called them to. Thus, they took them as lords (أَرْباباً) through their obedience to them and their abandonment of the command of Allah, His Books, and His Messengers, casting them behind their backs." He (peace be upon him) said: "This was only mentioned in Our Book so that we may take heed from them." وَما أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا ** though they were commanded only to worship to obey إِلهاً واحِداً ** One God. and He is Allah, the Exalted. As for the obedience to the Messengers and their successors (أوصيائهم), it is, in reality, obedience to Allah, because they command and forbid on behalf of Allah. لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ سُبْحانَهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** There is no god except Him. Exalted is He above what they associate [with Him]! A declaration of His transcendence (تنزيه) above association (الاشراك).

(٣٢) يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُطْفِؤُا They extinguish/put out نُورَ اللهِ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ (١) with their polytheism (شرك) and their denial (تكذيب) وَيَأْبَى اللهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ by exalting Monotheism (التوحيد) and strengthening Islam (الإسلام) وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكافِرُونَ ** (32) They desire to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah is intent on perfecting His light, though the faithless should be averse. Allah, glory be to Him, likened their state in seeking to invalidate the prophethood of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali عليه السلام through denial, to the state of one who desires to blow upon a great light—which Allah intends to bring to the utmost limit of illumination and radiance—in order to extinguish it with his breath.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام regarding this Ayah: It means that they affirmed in the Book what Allah had not said, so that they might confuse the creation. But Allah blinded their hearts until they left in it what indicated what they had innovated therein and what they had distorted from it.

And narrated from Him عليه السلام: He made the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), those who uphold it and are knowledgeable of its apparent and hidden meanings, to be from a tree whose root is firm and whose branches are in the sky, yielding its fruit in every season by the leave of its Lord (meaning: such knowledge appears to those who bear it time after time). And He made its enemies the people of the accursed tree, who attempted to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah was intent إِلَّا أَنْ يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ. (on perfecting His light).

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He mentioned that Pharaoh split open the bellies of pregnant women in search of Moses. Likewise, Banu Umayyah (بنو أميّة) and Banu al-Abbas (بنو العبّاس), when they realized that the demise of the kingdom of the princes and tyrants among them would be at the hands of the Qā’im (القائم), they showed enmity towards us and drew their swords to kill the Household (أهل بيت) of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and annihilate his progeny, hoping thereby to reach and kill the Qā’im عليه السلام. But Allah refused to reveal His affair to any of the oppressors إِلَّا أَنْ يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ. (except that He perfects His light, though the polytheists should be averse).

(٣٣) هُوَ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالْهُدى وَدِينِ الْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ (33) He it is who sent His Apostle with the guidance and the religion of truth, that He may make it prevail over all religions, so that the religion of truth prevails over all other religions وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ. (even if the polytheists should be averse.)

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Qā’im (القائم) from the Household of Muhammad (peace be upon him and upon them). He said: And this is what We mentioned regarding that whose interpretation (تأويله) comes after its revelation (تنزيله).

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "By Allah, its interpretation (تأويلها) has not yet come down, nor will its interpretation come down until the Qā’im (القائم) emerges. When the Qā’im (peace be upon him) emerges, there will remain no disbeliever in Allah the Almighty, nor any polytheist (مشرك) in the Imam (بالإمام), except that he will dislike his emergence. Even if a disbeliever or polytheist were inside the belly of a rock, the rock would say: 'O believer, there is a disbeliever in my belly, so break me and kill him.'"

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "He is the One Who commanded His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with the Wilāyah (الولاية) for His successor (لوصيّه), and the Wilāyah (الولاية) is the religion of truth (دين الحقّ), so that He may make it prevail over all religions upon the rising of the Qā’im (peace be upon him). And Allah will complete the Wilāyah (ولاية) of the Qā’im وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكافِرُونَ ** (even if the disbelievers should be averse) to the Wilāyah (بولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him)." It was asked: "Is this a revelation (تنزيل)?" He replied: "Yes, this phrase (الحرف) is a revelation (تنزيل), but what is other than it is interpretation (تأويل)."

And therein (al-Kafi), in the narration of the intimate conversation (مناجاة) of Moses (peace be upon him) with his Lord, He mentioned Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "My words are fulfilled: I shall surely make His religion prevail over all religions, and I shall surely be worshipped in every place."

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "And the Master of this Affair (صاحب هذا الأمر) will be absent due to the clarification of treachery (الغدر) against him in that matter, because of the encompassing nature of the trial (الفتنة) over the hearts, until the people closest to him become the most severe in enmity towards him. At that time, Allah will support him بِجُنُودٍ لَمْ تَرَوْها ** (with hosts you did not see) and He will make the religion of His Prophet prevail through his hands عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ.** (over all religions, even if the polytheists should be averse.)"

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "That will occur upon the emergence of the Mahdi (المهديّ) from the Household of Muhammad (upon him and upon them be the blessings of Allah), and no one will remain except that he acknowledges Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) a narration with the same meaning. He (peace be upon him) said: And in another report, he said: "That Allah may make it prevail (لِيُظْهِرَهُ) during the Return (في الرّجعة)." And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) that he said: "Has that prevailed yet?" They said: "Yes." He said: "No, by the One in Whose hand is my soul, not until there remains no village except that it calls out the testimony that there is no god but Allah and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, morning and evening."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He said: "When the Qā’im (القائم) emerges, there will remain no polytheist (مشرك) in Allah the Almighty, nor any disbeliever (كافر), except that he will dislike his emergence."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "There will remain no house of clay (مدر) (1) nor of camel hair (وبر) (2) upon the face of the earth except that Allah will cause Islam to enter it, either by the might of the mighty or the humiliation of the abased. Either He will honor them, and Allah will make them among its people, so they are honored by it, or He will humiliate them, so they submit to it."

And in al-Ikmal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The Qā’im (القائم) from us is aided by awe (منصور بالرّعب), supported by victory (مؤيّد بالنّصر). The earth will be folded for him (تطوى له الأرض), and the treasures will appear for him. His authority will reach the East and the West, and Allah will make His religion prevail through him over all religions. No ruin (خراب) will remain on earth except that it will be rebuilt, and the Spirit of Allah, Jesus son of Mary, will descend and pray behind him." (The rest of the narration is implied by الحديث).

footnote (1) Because extinguishing (*الإطفاء*) is done by mouths, which is blowing (*النّفخ*). This is among the wonders of eloquence, along with the belittling of their status and the weakening of their cunning, because the mouth affects weak lights, not great measures (*الأقياس العظيمة*). (*Majma' al-Bayan*).

(٣٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنَ الْأَحْبارِ وَالرُّهْبانِ لَيَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ النَّاسِ بِالْباطِلِ (34) O you who have faith! Indeed many of the scribes and monks wrongfully eat up people’s wealth, They take it from the unlawful (الحرام) through bribes (الرّشاءِ) in judgments (الأحكام) and by lightening the religious laws (الشرايع) for the common people (العوام). وَيَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ meaning, from His religion (دينه). وَالَّذِينَ يَكْنِزُونَ الذَّهَبَ وَالْفِضَّةَ وَلا يُنْفِقُونَها فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ He restricted the hoarding (الكنز) by the lack of spending, so that it does not include the one who gathered [wealth] for the purpose of spending, and [it is restricted] after the rights (الحقوق) have been extracted. فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ so inform them of a painful punishment. It is the branding (الكيّ) with them.

The Almighty's saying: **يَوْمَ يُحْمى عَلَيْها فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوى بِها جِباهُهُمْ وَجُنُوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ ** (35) the day when these [treasures] are heated in the fire of hell, and their brows, sides, and backs are branded with them:

The fire possessing intense heat (حَمى) is kindled upon the treasures فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوى بِها ** with those heated treasures جِباهُهُمْ وَجُنُوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ . It was said that these specific limbs were singled out because, by abandoning spending (in charity), they only sought to preserve worldly aims: prestige among people, ensuring their honor (ماء وجوههم) remained protected, enjoying delicious foods until they were completely satiated (يتضلّعون) (3), and wearing soft clothes which they cast upon their backs. Or, because they would frown their faces (يعبسون وجوههم) at the poor person when they saw him, turn their sides (جنوبهم) away from him, and if he circled around, they would give him their backs (ظهورهم). Furthermore, the brows (الجباه) are a metaphor for the front parts of the body, the sides (الجنوب) for its extremities, and the backs (الظهور) for the rear parts, meaning that the branding encompasses the entire body. هذا ما كَنَزْتُمْ ** meaning, it will be said to him: "This is what you hoarded for yourselves (لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ)," for the benefit of your souls, and it became the cause of their torment. فَذُوقُوا ما كُنْتُمْ تَكْنِزُونَ ** meaning, its evil consequence (وباله).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: Allah has forbidden the hoarding (كنز) of gold and silver and commanded their expenditure in the way of Allah (في سبيل الله). He said: Abu Dharr al-Ghifari used to go out every day while he was in Sham (الشام) and call out with the loudest voice: "Give glad tidings to the people of the hoards (الكنوز) of branding upon the brows (الجباه), branding upon the sides (الجنوب), and branding upon the backs (الظهور), until the heat circulates within their bellies (أجوافهم)."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace): When this Ayah was revealed, He said: "Woe to gold! Woe to silver!" repeating it three times. This was difficult for his Companions, so 'Umar asked Him: "Which wealth should we acquire?" He replied: "A remembering tongue (لساناً ذاكراً), a grateful heart (قلباً شاكراً), and a believing wife who assists one of you in his religion."
