(٧٨) أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّهُمْ ** (78) Do they not know that Allah knows their secret what they concealed within themselves of hypocrisy (النفاق) وَنَجْواهُمْ ** and their secret talks, and what they secretly converse about among themselves concerning the slanders (المطاعِن) **وَأَنَّ اللهَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ ** and that Allah is the Knower of the Unseen? Nothing is hidden from Him.

(٧٩) الَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ الْمُطَّوِّعِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الصَّدَقاتِ وَالَّذِينَ لا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ (79) Those who find fault with the voluntary donors among the faithful regarding the charities, and those who find nothing [to give] except their meager means,

الَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ ** means: they criticize (يعيبون) الْمُطَّوِّعِينَ ** meaning: the voluntary donors (المتطوّعين) مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الصَّدَقاتِ وَالَّذِينَ لا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ ** meaning: except their capacity (طاقتهم), so they give charity with little.

And in the Hadith: The best charity is the effort (جهد) of the poor person (المقلّ).

فَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنْهُمْ سَخِرَ اللهُ مِنْهُمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ and ridicule them—Allah will ridicule them, and there is a painful punishment for them.

فَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنْهُمْ ** means: they mock (يستهزِؤُن). سَخِرَ اللهُ مِنْهُمْ ** means: He recompensed them with the recompense of mockery (جازاهم جزاء السّخرية). This is according to al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him). **وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ **

Al-Qummi (reports): Salim ibn Umayr al-Ansari came with a sa' (صاع) of dates and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I spent my night pulling the rope (أجرّ الجرير) (1) until I earned two sa's (صاعين) of dates. As for one of them, I kept it, and as for the other, I loaned it to my Lord." The Messenger of Allah صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that it be scattered among the charities. The hypocrites mocked him and said: "By Allah, Allah is certainly rich enough (لغنيّ) without this sa'. What does Allah do with his sa' of dates? But Abu Aqil only wanted to mention himself so that he might be given from the charities." So, [the verse] was revealed.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) hired himself out to draw water, receiving one date for every bucket, choosing the best dates. He collected dates and brought them to the Prophet صلىّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf was at the door and criticized him (فلمزه), meaning: he spoke ill of him (وقع فيه). So, this verse was revealed: الَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ.

(1) Al-Jarir (الجرير) is the rope with which the camel is pulled. [The narrator] means that he drew water for the people for the wage of two sa's (صاعين). "From him (may Allah have mercy on him)."

(80) اسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ أَوْ لا تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ (Whether you plead for forgiveness for them or do not plead for forgiveness for them,) There is no difference between the two matters in terms of their lack of benefit for them. إِنْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ (if you plead for forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah will never forgive them.) It was said that the number seventy (السبعون) came in their speech as a common idiom (مجرى المثل) for indicating multiplicity (للتكثير). And the general populace (العامّة) narrated that He صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By Allah, I shall certainly increase upon seventy," so the verse was revealed: سَواءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ لَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ (It is the same for them whether you plead for forgiveness for them or do not plead for forgiveness for them: Allah will never forgive them.) And in another wording, He said: "Had I known that if I increased upon seventy times, He would forgive them, I would have done so."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): That Allah said to Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: إِنْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ (if you plead for forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah will never forgive them). So, plead for forgiveness for them a hundred times so that He may forgive them. Then Allah revealed: سَواءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ لَهُمْ (It is the same for them whether you plead for forgiveness for them) — the Ayah. And He said: وَلا تُصَلِّ عَلى أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ ماتَ أَبَداً وَلا تَقُمْ عَلى قَبْرِهِ (Do not ever pray over any of them who dies, nor stand by his grave.) So, He did not plead for forgiveness for them after that, nor did He stand by the grave of any one of them.

I say: It is not unlikely that the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم sought forgiveness for those among the disbelievers whose faith He hoped for. However, it is only impermissible for Him to seek forgiveness for those whose faith He despaired of. And this is His saying, exalted is His glory: ما كانَ لِلنَّبِيِّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْ يَسْتَغْفِرُوا لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَلَوْ كانُوا أُولِي قُرْبى مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحابُ الْجَحِيمِ (It is not for the Prophet and the faithful to plead for forgiveness for the polytheists, even if they are kinsmen, after it has become clear to them that they are the inmates of the Hellfire.) — up to His saying: تَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُ (he disowned him.) The full discussion concerning this matter will come shortly, if Allah wills. ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ (That is because they disbelieved in Allah and His Apostle,) This is an indication that the despair of forgiveness and the non-acceptance of your plea for forgiveness is not due to stinginess (بخل) on Our part, nor due to any deficiency in you, but rather due to their lack of receptivity (قابليّتهم) because of the disbelief (الكفر) which turns them away from it. وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْفاسِقِينَ (and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot.) — those who are rebellious (المتمردين) in their disbelief.

(٨١) فَرِحَ الْمُخَلَّفُونَ بِمَقْعَدِهِمْ خِلافَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ** (81) Those who were left behind rejoiced at their sitting back, contrary to [the command of] the Apostle of Allah, [They rejoiced] at their sitting back from the campaign (الغزو) and [sitting back] after him (خلفه). It is said: أقام خلاف القوم (He stayed behind the people), meaning: after them (بعدهم). وَكَرِهُوا أَنْ يُجاهِدُوا بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** and they were averse to striving with their possessions and their lives in the way of Allah. [This was] preferring ease (الدّعة) and comfort (الخفض) (1) over obedience to Allah. وَقالُوا لا تَنْفِرُوا فِي الْحَرِّ ** And they said, 'Do not go forth in the heat.' Some of them said this to others. The story of الجد بن قيس regarding this has already preceded during the exegesis of: وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَقُولُ ائْذَنْ لِي ** (And among them is he who says, 'Give me leave'). And this is a disgrace (تفضيح) for him from Allah, the Glorious. قُلْ نارُ جَهَنَّمَ أَشَدُّ حَرًّا ** Say, 'The fire of hell is severer in heat,' And you have preferred it through this disobedience. If they were to comprehend (يَفْقَهُونَ) that their destination is towards it and what it is like, they would not have chosen it by preferring ease (الدعة) over obedience (الطاعة).

(٨٢) فَلْيَضْحَكُوا قَلِيلاً وَلْيَبْكُوا كَثِيراً ** (82) So let them laugh a little, and weep much, Either according to the apparent meaning of the command (الأمر), or it is information (أخبار) about what their condition will ultimately lead to in this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخرة). Meaning, they will laugh little and weep much. He brought it forth in the form of a command (صيغة الأمر) to indicate that it is an inevitable, obligatory matter (حتم واجب). And it is permissible that the laughing (الضحك) and the weeping (البكاء) are metaphors (كنايتين) for joy (السرور) and sorrow (الغمّ). جَزاءً بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (82) as a requital for what they used to earn. from disbelief (الكفر) and holding back (التخلف).

(٨٣) فَإِنْ رَجَعَكَ اللهُ إِلى طائِفَةٍ مِنْهُمْ ** (83) If Allah brings you back to a section of them, This means if He returns you to al-Madinah (المدينة), and in it is a section (طائفة) of those who lagged behind (المتخلّفين), meaning the hypocrites (المنافقيهم) among those who did not repent and did not have a valid excuse (عذر صحيح) for lagging behind (التخلف). فَاسْتَأْذَنُوكَ لِلْخُرُوجِ ** and they ask your permission to go out, to another campaign (غزوة أخرى) after Tabuk. فَقُلْ لَنْ تَخْرُجُوا مَعِيَ أَبَداً وَلَنْ تُقاتِلُوا مَعِيَ عَدُوًّا ** say, 'You shall never go out with me, nor shall you fight any enemy along with me.' This is a statement (اخبار) carrying the meaning of prohibition (النهي) for emphasis (للمبالغة). إِنَّكُمْ رَضِيتُمْ بِالْقُعُودِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** Indeed, you were pleased with staying behind the first time. This is the justification (تعليل) for it. Their removal (اسقاطهم) from the register of warriors (ديوان الغزاة) was a punishment (عقوبة) for them due to their lagging behind (تخلّفهم) the first time, which was the expedition (الخرجةِ) to the campaign of Tabuk (غزوة تبوك). فَاقْعُدُوا مَعَ الْخالِفِينَ ** So stay behind with those who stay behind." Meaning, those who lagged behind (المتخلّفين) due to their unsuitability (لعدم لياقتهم) for Jihad (الجهاد), such as women (النّساءِ) and children (الصّبيان).

(٨٤) وَلا تُصَلِّ عَلى أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ ماتَ أَبَداً ** (And never pray over any of them who dies) Do not supplicate for him or seek forgiveness for him, وَلا تَقُمْ عَلى قَبْرِهِ ** (nor stand by his grave) for supplication.

In al-Majma' (مجمع البيان): For when he (peace be upon him) would pray over a deceased person, he would stand by his grave for an hour and supplicate for him. Then Allah forbade him from praying over the hypocrites, standing by their graves, and supplicating for them. Then He clarified the reason for both matters: إِنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَماتُوا وَهُمْ فاسِقُونَ. (Indeed, they disbelieved in Allah and His Apostle, and they died while they were wicked.)

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): Regarding the previous verse concerning seeking forgiveness (آية الاستغفار), it was revealed when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned to Medina and 'Abd Allah ibn Ubayy fell ill. His son, 'Abd Allah, was a believer, so he came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while his father was on his deathbed. He said: "O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you! If you do not come to my father, that would be a disgrace (عاراً) upon us." So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered upon him while the hypocrites were with him.

His son, 'Abd Allah ibn 'Abd Allah, said: "O Messenger of Allah, seek forgiveness for him." So, He sought forgiveness. 'Umar then said: "Has Allah not forbidden you, O Messenger of Allah, from praying over them or seeking forgiveness for them?"

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم turned away from him, but he repeated it. So, He said to him: "Woe unto you (وَيلك)! I was given a choice, and I chose. Indeed, Allah says: **اسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ أَوْ لا تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ إِنْ تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَهُمْ ** (Whether you plead for forgiveness for them or do not plead for forgiveness for them, even if you plead for forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah will never forgive them.)" When 'Abd Allah died, his son came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

He said: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah! If you deem it fit to attend his funeral (جنازته)." So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم attended. He stood by his grave, and 'Umar said to Him: "O Messenger of Allah, has Allah not forbidden you from praying over any of them who dies, ever, and from standing by his grave?"

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "Woe unto you (ويلك)! Do you know what I said? I only said: 'O Allah, fill his grave with fire, and his belly with fire, and make him enter (أصْلِهِ) (١) the Fire.'" Thus, something became apparent from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he did not wish to reveal.

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to the son of 'Abd Allah ibn Ubayy: "When you are finished with your father, inform me." He had already passed away, so the son came and informed Him. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took His sandals to stand up (to leave/attend). 'Umar said to Him: "Has Allah not said: وَلا تُصَلِّ عَلى أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ ماتَ أَبَداً وَلا تَقُمْ عَلى قَبْرِهِ (And never pray over any of them who dies, ever, nor stand by his grave)?" He replied: "Woe unto you (ويحك) or Woe unto you (ويلك)! I only say: 'O Allah, fill his grave with fire, and fill his belly with fire, and make him enter (واصْلِهِ) the Fire on the Day of Resurrection.'" In another narration, He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took the hand of his son during the funeral and proceeded. 'Umar confronted Him and then said: "Has your Lord not forbidden you from this: from praying over any of them who dies, ever, or from standing by his grave?" He did not answer him. When they were about to reach the grave, 'Umar repeated what he had said earlier.

The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then said to 'Umar: "Did you see us pray over his funeral (جنازة) or stand by his grave?" Then He said: "Indeed, his son is a man among the believers, and it was incumbent upon us to fulfill his right (أداء حقّه)." 'Umar said: "I seek refuge in Allah from the wrath (سخط) of Allah and your wrath, O Messenger of Allah."

I say: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was modest (حيّياً) and noble, just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَيَسْتَحْيِي مِنْكُمْ وَاللهُ لا يَسْتَحْيِي مِنَ الْحَقِ (and He is ashamed of you, and Allah is not ashamed of the truth). Thus, He disliked that a man among His companions who outwardly displayed faith should be exposed (يفتضِحَ). He would supplicate against the hypocrites while using tawriyah (يوري) (١) (ambiguous speech) to make it seem as if He was supplicating for them. This is the meaning of His saying صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to 'Umar: "Did you see us pray over his funeral or stand by his grave?" Likewise, this is the meaning of His saying صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in the narration of al-Qummi: "I was given a choice, and I chose." So, He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used tawriyah (ورّى) by choosing to seek forgiveness. As for the statement in that narration, "So, He sought forgiveness for him," perhaps He sought forgiveness for the son when the son requested forgiveness for his father, while He knew that the father was among the inhabitants of Hell (أصحاب الجحيم). What we have stated is indicated by the saying (peace be upon him): "Thus, something became apparent from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he did not wish to reveal."

If the narration of al-Qummi is authentic, then it is because it was not attributed to the Infallible (المعصوم). Reliance on the narration of al-Ayyashi here is greater than on the narration of al-Qummi because the former is attributed to the saying of the Infallible, unlike the latter. This is because the context of al-Qummi's speech sometimes indicates that the story of Ibn Ubayy was the reason for the revelation of the verse, and at other times indicates that it was revealed before that.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would pronounce the takbir (يكبّر) five times over some people and four times over others. If He pronounced the takbir four times over a man, it meant he was accused (of hypocrisy (النّفاق)).

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed over a deceased person, He would pronounce the takbir (كبّر) and recite the tashahhud (تشهد), then pronounce the takbir and send blessings upon the Prophets (الأنبياء), then pronounce the takbir and supplicate for the believers (المؤمنين), then pronounce the fourth takbir and supplicate for the deceased (الميّت), then pronounce the takbir and depart. When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, forbade Him from praying over the hypocrites, He would pronounce the takbir and recite the tashahhud, then pronounce the takbir and send blessings upon the Prophets (النّبّيين), then pronounce the takbir and supplicate for the believers (المؤمنين), then pronounce the fourth takbir and depart, and He would not supplicate for the deceased.

footnote (١) الخفض الراحة والسّكون يقال هو في خفض من العيش أي في سعة وراحة ومنه عيش خافض وعيش خفيض اي واسع م.

footnote (١) والصّلاء ككساء الشّواء لأنّه يصلى بالنّار والصّلاء أيضاً النّار قال الجوهريّ فان فتحت الصّاد قصرت وقلت صلا النّار والاصطلاء بالنّار التسخّن بها وفلان لا يصطلى بناره اي شجاع لا يطاق م.

footnote (١) ورّيت الخبر بالتشديد تورية إذا سترته وأظهرت غيره حيث يكون للّفظ معنيان أحدهما اشيع من الآخر فتنطق به وتريد الخفي م.

(٨٥) وَلا تُعْجِبْكَ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَأَوْلادُهُمْ إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ أَنْ يُعَذِّبَهُمْ بِها فِي الدُّنْيا ** (85) Let not their wealth and children impress you. Allah only desires to torment them with these in the life of the world, by what afflicts them therein of calamities (المصائب) and sorrows (الغموم), and by what is difficult for them regarding the expenditure of it for the Zakāt (الزّكوات) and spending in the way of Allah (في سبيل الله). وَتَزْهَقَ أَنْفُسُهُمْ وَهُمْ كافِرُونَ ** and that their souls may depart while they are faithless. The interpretation of the verse has already been mentioned, and it was only repeated for emphasis (للتأكيد), or this (verse) concerns a group (فريق) other than the first one.

(٨٦) وَإِذا أُنْزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَجاهِدُوا مَعَ رَسُولِهِ اسْتَأْذَنَكَ أُولُوا الطَّوْلِ مِنْهُمْ ** (86) When a surah is sent down [enjoining]: 'Believe in Allah and wage jihad along with His Apostle,' those of them who have means ask your leave and say: Those possessing grace (الفضل) and means (السّعة) وَقالُوا ذَرْنا نَكُنْ مَعَ الْقاعِدِينَ ** 'Leave us alone, we will be with those who stay behind.' those who stayed behind due to an excuse (عذر).

(٨٧) رَضُوا بِأَنْ يَكُونُوا مَعَ الْخَوالِفِ ** (They were content to be with those who stayed behind) (It is) the plural of خالفة. Al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, said: "with the women (النساء)." وَطُبِعَ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** (and a seal has been set on their hearts, so they do not understand.) [They do not understand] the happiness (السّعادة) found in Jihad (الجهاد) and conforming to the Messenger (الرسول), nor the wretchedness (الشّقاوة) found in staying behind from him.

(٨٨) **لكِنِ الرَّسُولُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ جاهَدُوا بِأَمْوالِهِمْ وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (88) But the Apostle and those who have faith with him waged jihad with their possessions and their lives.

If these (people) lagged behind and did not strive (يجاهدوا), then those who are better than them have striven.

**وَأُولئِكَ لَهُمُ الْخَيْراتُ ** And it is they who shall have the good things.

(These are) the benefits (منافع) of the religion (الدين) and the world (الدنيا): victory (النصر) and spoils (الغنيمة) in the world (الدنيا), and Paradise (الجنّة) and its bliss (نعيمها) in the Hereafter (الآخِرة).

وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ And it is they who are the felicitous.

The successful ones (الفائزون) in attaining their goals (بالمطالب).

(٨٩) أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. (89) Allah has prepared for them gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. That is the great success. (٩٠) وَجاءَ الْمُعَذِّرُونَ مِنَ الْأَعْرابِ ** the people of the desert (أهل البدو) لِيُؤْذَنَ لَهُمْ الْمُعَذِّرُونَ ** the negligent ones (المقصرون), derived from (عذر) in the matter when he was lax (توانى) and did not strive seriously (ولم يجدّ فيه). Its reality is that he pretends (يوهم) that he has an excuse (عذراً) for what he does, while he has no excuse (ولا عذر له). And it is permissible that it is derived from (اعتذر) (he excused himself) when he prepared the excuse (مهّد العذر), by merging the (التّاءِ) into the (الذال) and transferring its vowel movement to the (العَين). And these are those who excuse themselves with falsehood (بالباطل). وَقَعَدَ الَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** in claiming faith (ادعاءِ الإيمان), so they neither responded (فلم يجيبُوا) nor excused themselves (ولم يعتذرُوا). سَيُصِيبُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** by killing (بالقتل) and the Fire (والنّار). (90) And the excusers among the Bedouins came that they might be granted leave, and those who lied to Allah and His Apostle sat back. A painful punishment will befall those of them who disbelieved.

(٩١) لَيْسَ عَلَى الضُّعَفاءِ وَلا عَلَى الْمَرْضى ** (91) There is no blame on the weak, nor on the sick, such as the extremely old (الهرمى) (1) and the chronically ill (الزّمنى), وَلا عَلَى الَّذِينَ لا يَجِدُونَ ما يُنْفِقُونَ ** nor on those who do not find anything to spend, due to their poverty (لفقرهم), there is blame (حَرَجٌ) (meaning) sin (اثم) in delaying (the departure), إِذا نَصَحُوا لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** provided they are sincere to Allah and His Apostle. through faith (بالإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة) in secret (السِّرِّ) and in public (العلانية). ما عَلَى الْمُحْسِنِينَ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ There is no way [to blame] the good-doers, (meaning) no sin (لا جناح) and no reproach (ولا عتاب). وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ.** and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

(٩٢) وَلا عَلَى الَّذِينَ إِذا ما أَتَوْكَ ** (92) And there is no blame on those who, when they came to you Meaning: with you (i.e., to go with you) لِتَحْمِلَهُمْ قُلْتَ لا أَجِدُ ما أَحْمِلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ تَوَلَّوْا وَأَعْيُنُهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ الدَّمْعِ ** to be provided with mounts, and you said, 'I do not find any mount for you,' they turned back, while their eyes overflowed with tears, Meaning: their tears flowed, for the (مِنْ) is for clarification (للبيان), as if the entire eye was overflowing tears حَزَناً أَلَّا يَجِدُوا ** grieving that they could not find So that they would not find (لِئَلَّا يَجِدُوا) ما يُنْفِقُونَ ** anything to spend. in their campaign (مغزاهم).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from them both (peace be upon them): One of them was Abdullah ibn Yazid ibn Warqa' al-Khuza'i.

And in al-Qummi: Regarding the story of the Battle of Tabuk, the Weepers (البكّاؤن) came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they were seven: Nadr from Banu Amr ibn Awf; Salim ibn Umayr, who had witnessed Badr (the disagreement (الأخلاف) is concerning him); and from Banu Waqif, Harami ibn Umayr; and from Banu Harithah, Ulayyah ibn Zayd, who was the one who gave his honor (عِرضه) in charity. That was because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded charity, and the people began bringing it. So Ulayyah came and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I do not have anything with which to give charity, but I have made my honor (عِرضي) permissible (for anyone who might slander me)."

The Messenger of Allah said to him: "Allah has accepted your charity." And from Banu Mazin ibn al-Najjar, Abu Layla Abd al-Rahman ibn Ka'b; and from Banu Salamah, Amr ibn Ghunaymah; and from Banu Zurayq, Salamah ibn Sakhr; and from Banu al-Mu'izz, Madhirah ibn Sariyah al-Sulami. These are the ones who came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم weeping, and they said: "O Messenger of Allah, we do not have the strength to go out with you." So Allah revealed concerning them: لَيْسَ عَلَى الضُّعَفاءِ وَلا عَلَى الْمَرْضى ** (There is no blame on the weak, nor on the sick) up to His saying: أَلَّا يَجِدُوا ما يُنْفِقُونَ ** (that they could not find anything to spend). He said: These Weepers (البكّاؤن) only asked for sandals (نعلاً) to wear.

(٩٣) إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَسْتَأْذِنُونَكَ وَهُمْ أَغْنِياءُ رَضُوا بِأَنْ يَكُونُوا مَعَ **الْخَوالِفِ ** (93) The way is only against those who ask your leave while they are rich. They are content to be with the stay-at-homes.

He said: They were eighty men from various tribes. And الْخَوالِفِ (the stay-at-homes) means the women. وَطَبَعَ اللهُ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (And Allah has set a seal on their hearts) until they became heedless of the grievousness (وخامة) (1) of the consequence, so they do not know its unseen aspect (مغيبه).

footnote (١) *الهرم* (with a *حركة*), *المهرم*, and *المهرمة* mean the extreme limit of old age (*الكبر*).

٩٤ يَعْتَذِرُونَ إِلَيْكُمْ إِذا رَجَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ قُلْ لا تَعْتَذِرُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكُمْ قَدْ نَبَّأَنَا اللهُ مِنْ أَخْبارِكُمْ وَسَيَرَى اللهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلى عالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (94) They will offer excuses to you when you return to them. Say, 'Do not make excuses. We will never believe you. Allah has informed us of your affairs.' And Allah will see your conduct, and His Apostle. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, and He will inform you concerning what you used to do.'

يَعْتَذِرُونَ إِلَيْكُمْ ** regarding their staying behind إِذا رَجَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ ** from the campaign (الغزوة). قُلْ لا تَعْتَذِرُوا ** with false excuses (بالمعاذير الكاذبة). لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكُمْ ** We will never confirm you (or: deem you truthful). قَدْ نَبَّأَنَا اللهُ مِنْ أَخْبارِكُمْ ** He informed us through revelation (بالوحي) to His Prophet of some of your affairs, which is what is in your consciences (ضمائركم) of evil (الشّرّ) and corruption (الفساد). وَسَيَرَى اللهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ ** whether you repent from disbelief (الكفر) or persist in it. ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلى عالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ ** meaning, to Him. The attribute (الوَصْف) was placed in the position of the pronoun (الضّمير) to indicate that He is aware of their secrets and their public deeds, and nothing from their consciences (ضمائرهم) or their actions escapes His knowledge. فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** with reproach (التوبيخ) and punishment (العقاب).

(٩٥) سَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللهِ لَكُمْ إِذَا انْقَلَبْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ لِتُعْرِضُوا عَنْهُمْ ** (95) They will swear by Allah to you when you return to them, so that you may disregard them. So do not reproach them, but disregard them, and do not rebuke them. Indeed, they are filth (رِجْسٌ); rebuke, advice (النّصح), and reproach (العتاب) are of no benefit to them. There is no way to Allah [for] their purification. وَمَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ جَزاءً بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ.** and their refuge is hell, a requital for what they used to earn.

(٩٦) يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ لِتَرْضَوْا عَنْهُمْ ** (They swear to you so that you may be pleased with them) by their oaths, so that you may continue upon them what you used to do for them. فَإِنْ تَرْضَوْا عَنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللهَ لا يَرْضى عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْفاسِقِينَ ** (But if you are pleased with them, Allah is indeed not pleased with the transgressing people.) And your pleasure will not benefit them if Allah is wrathful toward them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever seeks the pleasure of Allah through the displeasure of people, Allah will be pleased with him and will make the people pleased with him. And whoever seeks the pleasure of people through the displeasure of Allah, Allah will be wrathful toward him and will make the people wrathful toward him."

Al-Qummi said: When the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم returned from Tabuk, his believing companions would confront the hypocrites and harm them. And they (the hypocrites) would swear to them that they were upon the truth and were not hypocrites, so that you (the believers) might turn away from them and be pleased with them. So Allah revealed **سَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللهِ لَكُمْ ** (They will soon swear by Allah to you) and the rest of the verse.

footnote (١) The ill consequence (*وخامة العاقبة*) is its badness, its lack of conformity, its heaviness, and its baseness.

(٩٧) الْأَعْرابُ ** are the people of the desert (أهل البدوا). أَشَدُّ كُفْراً وَنِفاقاً ** (more intense in disbelief and hypocrisy) than the people of the settled regions (أهل الحضر), due to their wildness (توحشهم), their harshness (قساوتهم), their coarseness (جفائهم), and their upbringing (نشوهم) far away from observing the scholars (مشاهدة العلماء) and hearing the Revelation (سماع التنزيل). وَأَجْدَرُ أَلَّا يَعْلَمُوا ** (and more apt not to know), meaning, and more deserving (وأحقّ) not to know حُدُودَ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ ** (the bounds of what Allah has sent down to His Apostle) concerning the laws (الشرايع), their obligatory duties (فرائضها), and their recommended practices (وسننها). وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ (And Allah is All-knowing); He knows every single person, whether they are people of the wool (أهل الوبر) or people of the clay (أهل المدر), حَكِيمٌ (All-wise) in what He afflicts their wrongdoers (مسيئهم) and their righteous ones (محسنهم) with, be it punishment (عقاباً) or reward (وثواباً).

(٩٨) وَمِنَ الْأَعْرابِ مَنْ يَتَّخِذُ ** (98) And among the Bedouins are those who regard (considers/counts) ما يُنْفِقُ ** (what he expends in the way of Allah and gives in charity) مَغْرَماً ** (a fine and a loss), since he does not reckon it with Allah nor hopes for a reward for it, but rather he spends out of hypocrisy (رياءً) and dissimulation (تقيّة). وَيَتَرَبَّصُ بِكُمُ الدَّوائِرَ (The turns of time, its obstacles, and its incidents), so that the matter may turn against you, and he may be relieved of spending. عَلَيْهِمْ دائِرَةُ السَّوْءِ This is an interjection (اعتراض) by way of supplication (بالدّعاءِ) against them in the manner of what they await, or it is information (إخبار) about the occurrence of what they await upon them. وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ He is All-hearing of what they say during hypocrisy (النفاق), عَلِيمٌ** All-knowing of what they conceal.

(٩٩) وَمِنَ الْأَعْرابِ مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَيَتَّخِذُ ما يُنْفِقُ قُرُباتٍ ** (99) But among the Bedouins are some who believe in Allah and the Last Day, and regard what they spend as means of proximity [to Allah] (This spending is) a means (سبب) of proximity (قربات).

عِنْدَ اللهِ وَصَلَواتِ الرَّسُولِ ** with Allah and the blessings of the Apostle. And a means (سبب) for His (the Messenger's) supplications (دعواته), because he (the Messenger) used to supplicate for those who gave charity (للمتصدّقين) with goodness (بالخير) and blessing (والبركة) and seek forgiveness (ويستغفر) for them.

أَلا إِنَّها قُرْبَةٌ لَهُمْ ** Look! It is indeed a means of proximity for them. A testimony (شهادة) from Allah for them regarding the soundness (بصحة) of their belief (معتقدهم) and a confirmation (وتصديق) of their hope (لرجائهم).

سَيُدْخِلُهُمُ اللهُ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ** Soon Allah will admit them into His mercy. A promise (وعد) for them that mercy (الرحمة) will encompass (باحاطة) them.

إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. A confirmation (تقرير) for them.

(١٠٠) وَالسَّابِقُونَ الْأَوَّلُونَ مِنَ الْمُهاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصارِ. (The first and foremost of the Emigrants and the Helpers) Al-Qummi (said): They are the Nuqaba’ (النقباء), and Abu Dharr (أبو ذرّ), and Al-Miqdad (المقداد), and Salman (سَلمان), and Ammar (عمّار), and whoever believed, affirmed the truth, and remained firm upon the Wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And in Nahj al-Balagha: The designation of al-Hijrah (الهجرة) does not apply to anyone except through the recognition (معرفة) of the Proof (الحجّة) on Earth. So whoever recognizes Him and affirms Him is an Emigrant (مهاجر). وَالَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُمْ بِإِحْسانٍ ** (and those who followed them in virtue) [Meaning] through faith (الإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة) until the Day of Resurrection. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "So He began with the first Emigrants (المهاجرين الأوَّلين) according to the degree of their precedence, then He followed with the Helpers (الأنصار), then He thirded with the Followers (التابعين) in virtue (بِإِحْسانٍ). Thus, He placed every group according to the measure of their degrees and stations with Him." رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْهُمْ ** (Allah is pleased with them) [Meaning] by accepting their obedience and being satisfied with their deeds, وَ رَضُوا عَنْهُ (and they are pleased with Him) due to what they attained of His religious (الدّينيّة) and worldly (الدّنيويّة) blessings. وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي تَحْتَهَا الْأَنْهارُ ** (and He has prepared for them gardens with streams running in them) And it was recited (وقرئ): min taḥtihā (من تحتها) [from beneath them], as it is in other places. خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ **(١). (to abide in them forever. That is the great success.)

(١) And its meaning is that if the inhabitants of the deserts (البوادي) are disbelievers (كفّاراً) and hypocrites (منافقين), then they are أَشَدُّ كُفْراً ** (more intense in disbelief) than the people of the settled regions (أهل الحضر), due to their distance from the places of knowledge (مواضع العلم), from hearing the proofs (الحجج), and from witnessing the miracles (المعجزات) and the blessings of the Revelation (الوحي). M. N. (م ن).

(١٠١) وَمِمَّنْ حَوْلَكُمْ From those around your town, meaning Medina, مِنَ الْأَعْرابِ مُنافِقُونَ وَمِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ This is grammatically conjoined (عطف) to مِمَّنْ حَوْلَكُمْ مَرَدُوا عَلَى النِّفاقِ This is an attribute (صفة) for the hypocrites, meaning they became skilled (تمهّروا) in it and practiced (تمرّنوا) it. لا تَعْلَمُهُمْ You do not know them by their specific persons (بأعيانِهِم). This confirms their skill (مهارتهم) in it, meaning they conceal themselves from you despite your intelligence (فطنتك) and the truthfulness of your insight (فراستك), due to their excessive avoidance (تحاميهم) of positions of doubt concerning their affair. نَحْنُ نَعْلَمُهُمْ And We are aware of their secrets (أسرارهم). سَنُعَذِّبُهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ In al-Jawami', this refers to the striking of their faces and backs by the angels when their souls are seized, and the punishment of the grave (عذاب القبر). ثُمَّ يُرَدُّونَ إِلى عَذابٍ عَظِيمٍ The punishment of the Fire (عذاب النار).

وَمِمَّنْ حَوْلَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَعْرابِ مُنافِقُونَ وَمِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ مَرَدُوا عَلَى النِّفاقِ لَا تَعْلَمُهُمْ نَحْنُ نَعْلَمُهُمْ سَنُعَذِّبُهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّونَ إِلَى عَذابٍ عَظِيمٍ (101) Among the Bedouins around you there are hypocrites, and among the people of Medina [too]. They are steeped in hypocrisy. You do not know them; We know them. We will punish them twice, then they shall be relegated to a great punishment.

(١٠٢) وَآخَرُونَ اعْتَرَفُوا بِذُنُوبِهِمْ خَلَطُوا عَمَلاً صالِحاً وَآخَرَ سَيِّئاً عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. (102) And others have confessed to their sins, having mixed a righteous deed with another evil one. Maybe Allah will relent to them. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

In al-Qummi and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Abu Lubabah ibn 'Abd al-Mundhir, and his story has preceded during the exegesis of لا تَخُونُوا اللهَ وَالرَّسُولَ ** (Do not betray Allah and the Apostle) from Surat al-Anfal.

And in al-Kafi and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Those are believing people who commit, within their faith, sins that the believers find fault with and dislike. So those are the ones (for whom): عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ. (Maybe Allah will relent to them.)

And al-'Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: He said: "The word ‘asā (عَسَى) from Allah is obligatory (واجب), and it was only revealed concerning our sinful followers (شيعتنا المذنبين)."

And in another narration: (It refers to) people who committed sins, such as the killing of Hamzah and Ja'far al-Tayyar, then they repented. Then he said: "And whoever kills a believer will not be granted success for repentance (لَمْ يُوَفَّقْ لِلتَّوْبَةِ), except that Allah does not cut off the hope (طمع) of His servants in Him, nor their expectation (رجاء) from Him." He (the Imam) or someone else said: "Indeed, the word ‘asā (عَسَى) from Allah is obligatory (واجب)."

(103) خُذْ مِنْ أَمْوالِهِمْ صَدَقَةً. (Take a charity from their possessions) Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed when Abu Lubabah was released and offered his wealth for charity. تُطَهِّرُهُمْ (It purifies them)—the charity, or you (purify them)—وَتُزَكِّيهِمْ بِها (and purify them thereby), meaning, you attribute them to purity (الزّكاء). And the purification (التزكيَة) is an exaggeration in purification (التّطهير) and an increase therein, or it is in the sense of growth (الانماءِ) and blessing (البركة) in the wealth. وَصَلِّ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (and pray for them) and show mercy to them by supplicating for them for the acceptance of their charities and other things. إِنَّ صَلاتَكَ سَكَنٌ لَهُمْ (Indeed, your prayer is a solace for them); their souls find rest in it, and their hearts are reassured by it. وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ (And Allah is All-hearing); He hears your supplication for them, عَلِيمٌ** (All-knowing); He knows what proceeds from them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: That when a people brought their charity to him, He would say: "O Allah, bless them (اللهمّ صلّ عليهم)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: That He was asked about this Ayah: "Is it applicable (جارية) in the days after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم?" He said: "Yes."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him عليه السلام: When the Ayah of Zakat was revealed: خُذْ مِنْ أَمْوالِهِمْ صَدَقَةً ** (Take a charity from their possessions), and it was revealed in the month of Ramadan, the Messenger of Allah commanded his caller, and he called out among the people: "Indeed, Allah has made Zakat obligatory upon you, just as He made the prayer (الصلوة) obligatory upon you." So Allah made obligatory upon them (Zakat) from gold and silver, and He made obligatory upon them the charity (الصّدقة) from camels, cattle, and sheep, and from wheat, barley, dates, and raisins. And he called them to that in Ramadan, and He pardoned them concerning what was besides that. He said: Then he did not interfere with any of their wealth until the year passed upon them the following year, so they fasted and broke the fast. So he commanded his caller, and he called out among the Muslims: "O Muslims, pay the Zakat on your wealth, (so that) your prayers (صلوتكم) may be accepted." He said: Then he dispatched the collectors of charity (عمّال الصّدقة) and the collectors of the land tax (عمّال الطسُوق) (1).

(104) أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ ** (Do they not know that Allah is He who accepts the repentance of His servants) if it is sound, وَيَأْخُذُ الصَّدَقاتِ** (and takes the charities) if they proceed from sincerity of intention. He accepts them with the acceptance of one who takes something to render its equivalent.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And the taking (الأخذ) is in the sense of acceptance from Him, as He said: وَيَأْخُذُ الصَّدَقاتِ (and takes the charities), meaning He accepts them from their rightful people and rewards them for them."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah says: 'There is nothing except that I have appointed someone other than Me to seize it, except for charity (الصدقة). For I receive it (أتلقّفها) (٢) with My Hand, receiving it quickly, such that a man gives charity with a date or half a date, and I nurture it for him just as a man nurtures his colt (فلوه) (٣) and his young camel (فصيله) (٤). Then he comes on the Day of Resurrection, and it is like Mount Uhud, or greater than Uhud.'"

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "I guarantee concerning my Lord that the charity (الصدقة) does not fall into the hand of the servant until it falls into the Hand of the Lord. And that is His saying: هُوَ يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَأْخُذُ الصَّدَقاتِ." (He accepts the repentance of His servants and takes the charities.)

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When He gave something to a beggar, He would kiss the hand of the beggar. When He was asked why He did that, He said: "Because it falls into the Hand of Allah before the hand of the servant." And He said: "There is nothing except that an angel is appointed over it, except for charity (الصدقة), for it falls into the Hand of Allah." The narrator said: "I think He would kiss the bread or the dirham."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "My father, when he gave charity with something, would place it in the hand of the beggar, then take it back from him, kiss it, and smell it, then return it to the hand of the beggar."

In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "When you hand something to a beggar, ask him to supplicate for you, for their supplication is answered concerning you, but it is not answered concerning themselves because they lie. And let the one who handed it return his hand to his mouth and kiss it, for Allah, the Exalted, takes it before it falls into his hand, just as the Almighty said: أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَأْخُذُ الصَّدَقاتِ وَأَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ (104)" (Do they not know that Allah is He who accepts the repentance of His servants and takes the charities, and that Allah is the All-clement, the All-merciful?) It is His nature to accept the repentance of the repentant and to bestow favor upon them.

(١٠٥) وَقُلِ اعْمَلُوا (And say: 'Act) whatever you wish فَسَيَرَى اللهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ (for Allah will see your conduct, and His Apostle and the faithful [will see it too]) whether it was good or evil.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He mentioned this Ayah and said: "By Allah, He is the Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him)."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ (and the faithful) are the Imams (peace be upon them).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): The like of it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "He meant us."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah and said: "It is not like this; rather, it is وَالْمَأْمُونُونَ (and the trusted ones), and we are the trusted ones (الْمَأْمُونُونَ)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "The deeds of the servants are presented to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم every morning—their righteous and their wicked deeds. So beware of them. And this is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَقُلِ اعْمَلُوا (And say: 'Act') [the rest of] the Ayah."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "Indeed, Allah is a witness on His earth, and the deeds of the servants are presented to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Why do you grieve the Messenger of Allah?" It was asked: "How do we grieve him?" He replied: "Do you not know that your deeds are presented to him? If he sees an act of disobedience therein, that grieves him. So, do not grieve the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, but rather gladden him."

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "Supplicate to Allah for me and for my family." He said: "Do I not do so? By Allah, your deeds are presented to me every day and night." The narrator said: "I found that astonishing." He (peace be upon him) said: "Do you not read the Book of Allah?" He recited: وَقُلِ اعْمَلُوا فَسَيَرَى اللهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ (And say: 'Act, for Allah will see your conduct, and His Apostle and the faithful [will see it too]). He said: "By Allah, He is Ali ibn Abi Talib."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The deeds of the servants are presented to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم every morning—their righteous and their wicked deeds. So beware, and let one of you be ashamed that an ugly deed be presented to his Prophet.

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): There is no believer who dies, nor disbeliever who is placed in his grave, until his deed is presented to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and so on, sequentially, up to the last of those whose obedience Allah has made obligatory upon the servants. That is His saying: وَقُلِ اعْمَلُوا فَسَيَرَى اللهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ (And say: 'Act, for Allah will see your conduct, and His Apostle and the faithful [will see it too]).

وَسَتُرَدُّونَ إِلى عالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ (and soon you will be returned to the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen) by death, فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (and He will inform you concerning what you used to do) by way of recompense.

(106) وَآخَرُونَ مُرْجَوْنَ ** (106) And others are held in abeyance They are delayed (مؤخّرون), meaning their affair is suspended (موقوف أمرهم), derived from (ارجأته) (I delayed him) when you postpone him (أخّرته). And it was read (مرجون) with the waw (بالواو), and it carries the same meaning (بمعناه), for the command of Allah concerning their affair: إِمَّا يُعَذِّبُهُمْ وَإِمَّا يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ ** whether He punishes them or relents toward them, and Allah is all-knowing, of their states (بأحوالهم), All-wise (حَكِيمٌ) in what He does with them.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding this verse: [They are] a people who were polytheists (مشركين) and killed believers like Hamzah and Ja'far and their likes (أشباههما). Then they entered Islam, affirmed the Oneness of Allah (فوحّدوا الله), and abandoned polytheism (الشّرك). But they did not recognize faith (الإيمان) with their hearts, so as to be among the believers (المؤمنين) for whom Paradise (الجنّة) becomes obligatory. Nor did they remain upon their denial (جحودهم) so as to disbelieve (فيكفروا) for whom the Fire (النّار) becomes obligatory. So they are upon that state: إِمَّا يُعَذِّبُهُمْ وَإِمَّا يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ. (whether He punishes them or relents toward them.)

(١٠٧) وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مَسْجِداً (107) And those who built a mosque And الذين (those who) was recited without the و (waw), because it is a story unto itself.

In al-Jawami', it is narrated: When Banu 'Amr ibn 'Awf built the Mosque of Quba (مسجد قباء) (2) and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed in it, their brothers, Banu Ghanm ibn 'Awf, envied them and said: "We will build a mosque to pray in, and we will not attend the congregation of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." So they built a mosque next to the Mosque of Quba (مسجد قباء). They said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while He was preparing for Tabuk: "We would like you to come to us and pray for us in it." He said: "I am on the verge of travel." When He returned from Tabuk, [the revelation] descended, and He sent someone who demolished the mosque and burned it, and He commanded that its place be made a refuse heap where carcasses and rubbish are thrown. ضِراراً (for harm), causing injury to the believers, the companions of the Mosque of Quba (مسجد قباء), وَكُفْراً (and disbelief), or strengthening the disbelief they concealed, وَتَفْرِيقاً بَيْنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and to divide the believers) who used to gather for prayer in the Mosque of Quba (مسجد قبا). They intended for them to disperse from it and for their unity (كلمتهم) to be fractured. وَإِرْصاداً (and as an ambush), meaning preparation, or anticipation, لِمَنْ حارَبَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ (for those who waged war on Allah and His Apostle before). Meaning Abu 'Amir (أبا عامر) (1) the Monk (الراهب). It was said they built it with the intention that Abu 'Amir (أبو عامر) would lead them in prayer there when he arrived from Syria (الشام).

In al-Jawami', [it is narrated] that he had become a monk during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) and wore sackcloth. When the Prophet arrived in Medina (المدينة), he envied Him and gathered the confederates (الأحزاب) against Him. Then he fled after the Conquest of Mecca (فتح مكّة) and went out to the Romans (الرّوم) and became a Christian. These people were anticipating his return to them and prepared this mosque for him so that he could pray in it and prevail over the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And indeed, he used to fight the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in His campaigns until he fled to Syria (الشام) to bring soldiers from Caesar to fight the Messenger of Allah. He died alone in Qinnasrin (قنسرين) (2). وَلَيَحْلِفُنَّ إِنْ أَرَدْنا إِلَّا الْحُسْنى (And they will surely swear: 'We intended nothing but good.') We intended by its construction nothing but the good quality (الخصلة الحُسنى), which is prayer, remembrance (الذكر), and providing ease for the worshippers. وَاللهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ (But Allah bears witness that they are certainly liars) regarding their oath.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): The reason for its revelation was that a group of hypocrites came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, will You permit us to build a mosque in Banu Salim for the sick, the rainy night, and the frail old man?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم permitted them while He was preparing to depart for Tabuk. They said: "O Messenger of Allah, if only You would come to us and pray in it." He said: "I am on the verge of travel, but when I return, Allah willing, I will come to it and pray in it."

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم approached [returning] from Tabuk, this verse descended upon Him concerning the matter of the mosque and Abu 'Amir (أبي عامر) the Monk (الرّاهب). They had sworn to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that they were building it for righteousness (الصّلاح) and goodness (الحسنى). So Allah revealed to His Messenger: وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مَسْجِداً (And those who built a mosque...) [the rest of] the verse. He said: وَإِرْصاداً لِمَنْ حارَبَ اللهَ (and as an ambush for those who waged war on Allah) means Abu 'Amir (أبا عامر) the Monk (الرّاهب), who used to come to them and mention the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and His Companions.

And in Tafsir al-Imam (تفسير الإِمام) عليه السلام: Regarding His saying وَلَا تَقُولُوا رَاعِنَا (and do not say rā‘inā) from Surat al-Baqarah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to receive news from the ruler of Dumat al-Jandal (دومة الجندل) (1), who was the king of the surrounding regions and had a great kingdom adjacent to Syria (الشام). He used to threaten the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with attacking Him and killing His Companions, and the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were fearful and apprehensive of his heart.

He said: Then the hypocrites agreed and pledged allegiance (بايعُوا) to Abu 'Amir (أبي عامر) the Monk (الرّاهب), whom the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم named the Transgressor (الفاسق). They made him their commander (أميراً) and promised him obedience. He told them: "The best course of action is for me to be absent from Medina (المدينة) so that I am not suspected until your plan is complete." They corresponded with Ukaydir (أكَيْدِر), the ruler of Dumat al-Jandal (دومة الجندل), asking him to march toward Medina (المدينة). So Allah revealed to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, informing Him of what they had unanimously planned concerning His affair, and commanded Him to march to Tabuk. Whenever the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intended a campaign (غزوا), He would conceal His true destination with another, except for the Campaign of Tabuk (غزاة تبوك), for which He openly declared His intention and commanded them to prepare provisions. This was the campaign in which the hypocrites were exposed, and Allah condemned them for holding back from it. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم revealed what Allah the Almighty had revealed to Him: that Allah would grant Him victory over Ukaydir (أكيْدر) until He captured him and made peace with him for a thousand uqiyyah (أوقية) of gold in Rajab, two hundred garments (حلّة), and a thousand uqiyyah in Safar, and that He would return safely within eighty days.

The Messenger of Allah said to them: "Musa (Moses) promised his people forty nights, and I promise you eighty nights. I shall return safe, victorious, and triumphant, without any war taking place, and none of the believers shall be harmed."

The hypocrites said: "No, by Allah, but this is the last such honor (كرامة) after which there will be no recovery. Indeed, some of His companions will die in this heat, the winds of the deserts (البوادي), and the harmful, corrupted waters of the places. And whoever survives that will be either a captive in the hands of Ukaydir (أكيدر), or killed, or wounded." The hypocrites sought permission from Him, citing excuses: some pleaded the heat, some a sickness in their body, and some a sickness in their family. He used to grant them permission. When morning came and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم clearly resolved to depart for Tabuk, these hypocrites deliberately built a mosque outside Medina (المدينة), which is the Mosque of Harm (مسجد الضّرار). They intended to gather in it, giving the impression that it was for prayer, but it was only so they could meet under the pretext of prayer, complete their planning, and carry out there what would facilitate their desires. Then a group of them came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, our houses are far from the mosque, and we dislike praying without congregation (جماعة), and it is difficult for us to attend. We have built a mosque, so if You would visit it and pray in it, we may seek good fortune (نتيمّن) and blessing (نتبرّك) by praying in the place where You pray."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not inform them of what Allah had informed Him concerning their affair and their hypocrisy. He said: "Bring me my donkey." So al-Ya'fur (اليعفور) was brought, and He mounted it, intending to go toward their mosque. But every time He and His Companions urged it forward, it would not move or walk. However, when He turned its head away from it toward another direction, it walked its best and finest walk. They said: "Perhaps this donkey saw something on the road that it disliked, and that is why it does not move toward it."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Bring me a horse." So He mounted it. When He urged it toward their mosque, it would not move. Every time they tried to move it toward it, it would not stir, until they turned its head toward another direction, and it walked its best walk. They said: "Perhaps this horse disliked something on this road." So He said: "Come, let us walk to it." But when He and those with Him attempted to walk toward the mosque, they froze in their places and were unable to move. Yet, when they intended to go to other places, their movements became light, their bodies were purified, and their hearts were expanded.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "This is a matter that Allah has disliked and does not desire now. I am on the verge of travel, so grant me respite until I return, Allah willing, and then I will look into this with a consideration that pleases Allah." He then became earnest in His resolve to depart for Tabuk. The hypocrites resolved to destroy those left behind (مخلّفيهم) once He departed. So Allah the Almighty revealed to Him: "O Muhammad, the Most High, the Exalted (العليّ الأعلى), sends His greetings to you and says: Either you depart and 'Ali (عليّ) remains, or 'Ali (عليّ) departs and you remain." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "That is for 'Ali (عليّ)." 'Ali (عليّ) said: "Hearing and obedience to the command of Allah and the command of His Messenger, even though I love not to be left behind from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in any circumstance."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Are you not pleased that your status to Me is like the status of Harun (Aaron) to Musa (Moses), except that there is no prophet after Me?" He said: "I am pleased, O Messenger of Allah." The Messenger of Allah said to him: "O Abu al-Hasan, the reward for your departure with Me is in your remaining in Medina (المدينة). And Allah has made you an Ummah (أمّة) alone, just as He made Ibrahim (Abraham) an Ummah, such that your awe prevents the group of hypocrites and disbelievers from moving against the Muslims."

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed and 'Ali (عليّ) accompanied Him [for a distance], the hypocrites engaged in slander and said: "Muhammad only left him behind in Medina (المدينة) because He hates him and is tired of him. He only intended by this that the hypocrites should attack him by night and kill him." This reached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. 'Ali (عليّ) said: "Do You hear what they are saying, O Messenger of Allah?"

The Messenger of Allah said: "Is it not enough for you that you are the skin between My eyes, the light of My sight, and like the soul in My body?" Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم traveled with His Companions, and 'Ali (عليّ) remained in Medina (المدينة). Whenever the hypocrites planned to attack the Muslims, they were terrified of 'Ali (عليّ) and feared that he would rise against them, thus preventing them from their plan. They began saying among themselves: "This is Muhammad's last journey (كرّة) from which He will not return." Then [the narrator] mentioned the story of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with Ukaydir (أكيْدِر), His capture of him, and His peace treaty with him, as previously mentioned.

Then he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned victorious and triumphant, and Allah nullified the plot of the hypocrites. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded the burning of the Mosque of Harm (مسجد الضرّار). Then Allah the Almighty revealed the verses: وَالَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مَسْجِداً ضِراراً (And those who built a mosque for harm...) [the rest of] the verses. Then he mentioned that Abu 'Amir (أبا عامر) the Monk (الرّاهب) was the calf (عجل) of this Ummah (الأمّة), like the calf of the people of Musa (Moses), and that Allah destroyed him. He was afflicted with colic (قولنج), leprosy (برص), paralysis (فالج), and facial paralysis (لقوة), and remained for forty days in the severest torment, then he proceeded to the punishment of Allah.

footnote (١) قال الأزهري الأرجاء يهمّز ولا يهمّز يقال أرجأت الأمر وأرجيته.

footnote (٢) هو بضم القاف يقصر ويمدّ ولا يصرف ويذكّر ويؤنّث موضع بقرب المدينة المشرّفة من جهة الجنوب نحواً من ميلين وهو المسجد الذي **أُسِّسَ عَلَى التَّقْوى مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ** م.

footnote (١) وهو من اشراف قبيلة خزرج وله مهارة في علم التوراة والإِنجيل وكان يحدّث نعت النّبي على أهل المدينة فلمّا بعث النّبي صلّى الله عليه وآله وسلم وقدم بالمدينة حَسَدَه وهو أبو حنظلة غسيل الملائكة الّذي قتل مع النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله يوم احُد وكان جنباً فغسّله الملائكة.

footnote (٢) قِنسَرين وقنسرون بالكسر فيهما كورة بالشام وتكسر نونهما ق.

footnote (١) دومة الجندل حصن عادي بين المدينة والشّام يقرب من تبوك ثو هي اقرب إلى الشام وهي لفصل بين الشام والعراق وهي أحد حدود فدك ويقال انّها تسمّى بالجوف قال الجوهريّ وأصحاب اللغة يقولون بضم الدّال وأصحاب الحديث يفتحونها م.

(١٠٨) لا تَقُمْ فِيهِ أَبَداً ** (Do not stand in it ever) meaning, do not pray in it ever. It is said: "So-and-so stands (يَقُومُ) during the night," meaning he prays (يُصَلِّي). لَمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى التَّقْوى مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ (A mosque founded on God consciousness from the first day) from the days of its existence. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them), and in al-Qummi: It means the Mosque of Quba (مسجد قبا). It was said that the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم founded it and prayed in it during the days of his stay in Quba (قبا). أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَقُومَ فِيهِ ** (is worthier that you stand in it) worthier that you pray in it. And al-Ayyashi said: It means [worthier] than the Mosque of Hypocrisy (مسجد النفاق). There was a man on his way [to the Mosque of Quba] who, when he came to the Mosque of Quba and stood [to pray], would sprinkle water and lotus (السّدر) and lift his clothes above his shins and walk upon a stone on the side of the road, and he would hasten his walk, disliking that anything from it should touch his clothes. So I asked him: "Did the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم pray in the Mosque of Quba?" He said: "Yes." فِيهِ رِجالٌ يُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَتَطَهَّرُوا وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُطَّهِّرِينَ. (In it are men who love to purify themselves, and Allah loves those who purify themselves.) In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is purification after relieving oneself (الاستنجاء) with water. And al-Qummi [said]: They used to purify themselves (يَتطهّرون) with water. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir, from al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): يُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَتَطَهَّرُوا ** (who love to purify themselves) with water from feces (الغائط) and urine (البول). And narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: that He said to the people of Quba (قبا): "What do you do regarding your purification (طهركم), for Allah has praised you well?" They said: "We wash the trace of feces (الغائط)." So He said: "Allah revealed concerning you: وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُطَّهِّرِينَ.** (and Allah loves those who purify themselves.)"

(١٠٩) أَفَمَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيانَهُ عَلَى تَقْوَى مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانٍ (109) Is he who founded his building on the fear of Allah and His pleasure better, [Meaning] the building of his religion (بنيان دينه). عَلَى تَقْوَى مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانٍ (on the fear of Allah and His pleasure) [meaning] upon a firm foundation (قاعدة محكمة) which is the truth (الحقّ), and that is the fear (التقوى) of Allah and seeking His pleasure through obedience (الطّاعة). خَيْرٌ أَمْ مَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيانَهُ عَلَى شَفَا جُرُفٍ هَارٍ (better, or he who founded his building on the brink of a collapsing bank?) [meaning] upon a foundation (قاعدة) which is the weakest of foundations (القواعد) and the least enduring (أقلّها بقاءً), and that is falsehood (الباطل) and hypocrisy (النفاق), whose likeness is the likeness of the brink of a collapsing bank (شَفا جُرُفٍ هارٍ) in its lack of stability (قلّة الثبات). And al-shafā (الشّفا) is the edge (الشّفير), and juruf (جرف) is the side (جانبه) of the valley (الوادي) whose base (أصله) is hollowed out by water (الماءِ) and swept away by torrents (السيول). And al-hār (الهار) is the collapsing (الهاير) [bank] which is on the verge (أشفى) of falling (السقوط) and demolition (الهدم).

And it was read ussisa (أُسِّسَ) [founded] using the passive voice (البناءِ للمفعول), and juruf (جرف) with a lightened rā (بالتخفيف). فَانْهارَ بِهِ فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ (so it collapsed with him into the Fire of Hell). Since the juruf (الجرف) and the hār (الهار) were made a metaphor (مجازاً) for falsehood (الباطل), it was said فَانْهارَ بِهِ فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ (so it collapsed with him into the Fire of Hell). The meaning is: so falsehood (الباطل) plunged him فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ (into the Fire of Hell). It is as if the one who commits falsehood (المبطل) founded a building upon the edge (شفير) of Hell (جهنّم), so it tumbled with him into its depth (قعرها).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It refers to] the Mosque of Harm (مسجد الضرار) which was founded عَلى شَفا جُرُفٍ هارٍ (on the brink of a collapsing bank).

فَانْهارَ بِهِ فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** so it collapsed with him into the Fire of Hell. And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. [Meaning, He does not guide them] to that which contains righteousness (صلاح) and salvation (نجاة).

(١١٠) لا يَزالُ بُنْيانُهُمُ الَّذِي بَنَوْا (110) Their building which they built will never cease meaning the Mosque of Dissension (مسجد الضرار) رِيبَةً فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ** to be a source of doubt in their hearts, [it will be] a cause of doubt and increasing hypocrisy in their hearts, and its effect will not vanish. Then, when the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) demolished it, that [doubt] became firmly rooted in their hearts and increased, such that its trace (رسمه) will not disappear إِلَّا أَنْ تَقَطَّعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ ** unless their hearts are cut to pieces. [meaning] a cutting such that they no longer retain the capacity for perception (الإدراك) and concealment (الإضمار).

In al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited up to أَنْ تَقَطَّعَ ** (unless they are cut to pieces). And al-Qummi [narrated the reading] up to تَقَطَّعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ ** (their hearts are cut to pieces). And it was also read (نقطع) [in the active voice, meaning 'We cut']. وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ ** And Allah is all-knowing, of their intentions (بنيّاتهم), حَكِيمٌ ** all-wise, in what He commanded regarding the demolition of their building.

Al-Qummi [said]: So the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) sent Malik ibn Dukhthum al-Khuzā’ī and ‘Amir ibn ‘Adī, the brother of Banu ‘Amr ibn ‘Awf, to demolish it and burn it. So Malik came and said to ‘Amir, "Wait for me until I bring fire from my house." So he entered and came back with fire, and he ignited it in the palm fronds (سعف النّخل), then he ignited it in the mosque. So they dispersed, and Zayd ibn Harithah remained until the structure (البنية) was burned down, then he commanded the demolition of its wall (حائطه).

(١١١) إِنَّ اللهَ اشْتَرى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ ** (111) Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful their souls and their possessions, for Paradise shall be theirs. A metaphor for Allah's rewarding them with Paradise for their sacrificing their souls and possessions in His way. يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ ** They fight in the way of Allah, kill and are killed. This is an explanatory resumption clarifying the reason for which the purchase (الشرى) was made. And it was recited with the passive form (المبنى للمفعُول) being placed first. وَعْداً عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا فِي التَّوْراةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ وَالْقُرْآنِ ** A promise binding upon Him in the Torah and the Evangel and the Quran—a truthful promise. He promised that upon Himself, a firm, established promise recorded in the three Books. وَمَنْ أَوْفى بِعَهْدِهِ مِنَ اللهِ ** And who is truer to His promise than Allah? Meaning, no one. أَوْفى بِعَهْدِهِ مِنَ اللهِ فَاسْتَبْشِرُوا بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بايَعْتُمْ بِهِ ** So rejoice in the bargain you have made with Him. So rejoice in it with the utmost joy, since you have sold that which is perishable for that which is everlasting, and that which vanishes for that which is enduring. وَذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ.** That is the supreme success.

(١١٢) التَّائِبُونَ ** (1. The penitent ones) is in the nominative case (رفع) by way of praise, meaning: they are التَّائِبُونَ** (the penitent ones).

And in the recitation (قراءة) of al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): التّائبين (the penitent ones) up to His saying والحافظين (and the preservers). This was narrated in al-Majma' from them (peace be upon them both), applying it as an adjective (صفة) for the believers (المؤمنين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When التَّائِبُونَ الْعابِدُونَ ** (the penitent ones, the worshippers) was recited in his presence, he said: "No, I recite التائبين العابدين (the penitent ones, the worshippers) until the end of it." He was asked about the reason (العلّة) for this, and he replied: "He bought from the believers (اشْتَرى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) who are التّائبين العابدين (the penitent ones, the worshippers)." الْعابِدُونَ الْحامِدُونَ السَّائِحُونَ الرَّاكِعُونَ السَّاجِدُونَ الْآمِرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّاهُونَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَالْحافِظُونَ لِحُدُودِ اللهِ وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ.** (2. the worshippers, 3. the praisers, 4. the fasters, 5. the bowers, 6. the prostrators, 7. the enjoiners of the right and 8. the forbidder of the wrong, and 9. the preservers of Allah’s bounds. And give good news to the faithful.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When this verse was revealed: إِنَّ اللهَ اشْتَرى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful) a man stood up before the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Prophet of Allah, tell me about a man who takes his sword and fights until he is killed, yet he commits some of these forbidden acts (المحارم). Is he a martyr (شهيد)?" Then Allah revealed to His Messenger التَّائِبُونَ الْعابِدُونَ ** (the penitent ones, the worshippers) [the rest of] the verse. So the Prophet gave good tidings of martyrdom (الشهادة) and Paradise (الجنّة) to the Mujahidin among the believers whose description (صفتهم) and adornment (حليتهم) are these.

And He said: التَّائِبُونَ ** (The penitent ones) from sins; الْعابِدُونَ (the worshippers) are those who worship none but Allah and do not associate anything with Him; الْحامِدُونَ ** (the praisers) are those who praise Him in every state, in hardship (الشّدة) and ease (الرخاءِ); السَّائِحُونَ ** (the fasters) are the fasters (الصائمون); الرَّاكِعُونَ السَّاجِدُونَ ** (the bowers, the prostrators) are those who persevere in the five prayers (الصلوات الخمس), preserving them (الْحافِظُونَ لها) and maintaining them with their bowing (ركوعها), prostration (سجودها), humility (الخشوع) within them, and observing their proper times (أوقاتها); الْآمِرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ** (the enjoiners of the right) after that, and those who act upon it; and وَالنَّاهُونَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ (and the forbidder of the wrong) and those who abstain from it. He said: "So give good tidings of martyrdom and Paradise to whoever is killed while fulfilling these conditions." (The Hadith).

I say: The wandering (السياحة) was interpreted as fasting (الصيام) only because of the saying of the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The wandering (سياحة) of my community (أمتي) is fasting."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Abbad al-Basri met Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him) on the road to Mecca and said: "O Ali ibn al-Husayn, you have abandoned Jihad (الجهاد) and its difficulty, and turned toward Hajj (الحجّ) and its ease. إِنَّ اللهَ اشْتَرى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful) [the rest of] the verse." Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him) said to him: "Complete the verse." So he recited: التَّائِبُونَ الْعابِدُونَ ** (the penitent ones, the worshippers) [the rest of] the verse. Then Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them both) said to him: "If we see those whose description (صفتهم) is this, then Jihad with them is better than Hajj."

And al-Qummi [narrated]: Al-Zuhri met Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him) until the end of the Hadith.

Al-Ayyashi said: They are the Imams (الأئمّة) (peace be upon them).

And al-Qummi said: The verse was revealed concerning the Imams (peace be upon them), because He described them with a characteristic (صفة) that is not permissible for others. So الْآمِرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ** (the enjoiners of the right) are those who know all of the right (المعروف), its small and its large, its subtle (دقيقه) and its manifest (جليله). وَالنَّاهُونَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ ** (and the forbidder of the wrong) are those who know the wrong (المنكر), its small and its large. وَالْحافِظُونَ لِحُدُودِ اللهِ ** (and the preservers of Allah’s bounds) are those who know the bounds (حدود) of Allah, their small and their large, their subtle and their manifest. And it is not permissible for anyone other than the Imams (peace be upon them) to possess this characteristic (الصفّة).

And in Nahj al-Balagha: Indeed, there is no price for your souls except Paradise (الجنّة), so do not sell them for anything less than it.

And in it [i.e., Nahj al-Balagha]: You have neither sacrificed wealth (أموال) for the One Who provided it, nor risked souls (1) for the One Who created them.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That he was asked about the Almighty's saying **إِنَّ اللهَ اشْتَرى ** (Indeed Allah has bought) [the rest of] the verse.

He said: "It means during the Covenant (الميثاق)." Then التَّائِبُونَ الْعابِدُونَ ** (the penitent ones, the worshippers) was recited to him, and he said: "No, I recite it التائبين العابدين (the penitent ones, the worshippers) until the end of the verse." And he said: "When you see these people, then at that time, He bought their souls and their wealth from them," meaning during the Return (الرّجعة).

(١١٣) **ما كانَ لِلنَّبِيِّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْ يَسْتَغْفِرُوا لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَلَوْ كانُوا أُولِي قُرْبى مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحابُ الْجَحِيمِ ** (113) It is not for the Prophet and the faithful to ask forgiveness for the polytheists, even if they should be near relatives, after it has become clear to them that they are the inmates of the Hellfire.

[This clarity is achieved] by their death upon polytheism, or by a revelation from Allah that they will never believe.

(١١٤) وَما كانَ اسْتِغْفارُ إِبْراهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ إِلَّا عَنْ مَوْعِدَةٍ وَعَدَها إِيَّاهُ فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ أَنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ لِلَّهِ تَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُ (Abraham’s prayer for forgiveness for his father was only due to a promise he had made him; but when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he disowned him.)

He ceased seeking forgiveness for him.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said: "What do the people say regarding the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَما كانَ اسْتِغْفارُ إِبْراهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ (Abraham’s prayer for forgiveness for his father was only)?" It was said: "They say that Abraham promised his father that he would seek forgiveness for him." He said: "It is not like that. Rather, Abraham’s father promised him that he would submit (يُسْلِم), so he sought forgiveness for him. فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ أَنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ لِلَّهِ تَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُ (but when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he disowned him.)" And in another narration: When he died, it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, so he did not seek forgiveness for him.

I say: This interpretation does not contradict what al-Qummi narrated: that Abraham (peace be upon him) said to his father: "If you do not worship the idols, I will seek forgiveness for you." So when he did not abandon the idols, he disowned him. This is due to the possibility of both promises occurring, and that Abraham’s seeking forgiveness for him was conditional upon his submission (إسلامه), and that the intent of the promise (الوعد) in this verse is the promise of his father to him. And the saying of the Almighty indicates Abraham’s promise to him: إِلَّا قَوْلَ إِبْراهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ لَأَسْتَغْفِرَنَّ لَكَ إِنَّ إِبْراهِيمَ لَأَوَّاهٌ حَلِيمٌ. (except for Abraham’s saying to his father, ‘I shall plead with my Lord to forgive you. Indeed, Abraham is most plaintive and forbearing.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الأوّاه (the most plaintive) is the supplication (الدُّعاء).

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الأوّاه is the one who humbles himself before Allah in his prayer, and when he is alone in a desolate place (قفرة) on earth, and in solitude (الخلوات).

And it was said: He is the one who frequently sighs (التأوّه), weeps (البكاء), supplicates (الدّعاء), and frequently remembers Allah, exalted is His Name.

footnote (١) الخَطَر بالتّحريك الإشراف على الهلاك وقوله خاطر بنفسه من استغنى برأيه وبئس الخطر لمن خاطر الله بترك طاعته كلاهما من المخاطرة وهي ارتكاب ما فيه خطر وهلاك م. (1) الخَطَر (with al-tahrik) is being on the verge of destruction (الإشراف على الهلاك). And His saying: "He risked his soul (خاطر بنفسه) who was self-sufficient in his opinion," and "Wretched is the risk (الخطر) for the one who risks Allah (خاطر الله) by abandoning obedience to Him"—both are derived from المخاطرة (risking), which is undertaking that which involves danger (خطر) and destruction (هلاك). (م.)

(١١٥) وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُضِلَّ قَوْماً بَعْدَ إِذْ هَداهُمْ (115) And Allah is not one to lead astray a people after He has guided them, to Islam حَتَّى يُبَيِّنَ لَهُمْ ما يَتَّقُونَ until He has made clear to them what they should beware of. —that which must be guarded against (ما يجب اتقاؤه).

In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It means] until He makes known to them what pleases Him and what angers Him.

إِنَّ اللهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. —He knows their affair in both states.

(١١٦) **إِنَّ اللهَ لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ ** (116) Indeed, to Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and brings death, and besides Allah you do not have any guardian nor any helper.

It means that neither guardianship (ولاية) nor help (نصرة) is realized except from Allah. So, turn with your entire being towards Him, and disassociate yourselves from what is besides Him.

(117) The Almighty's saying: لَقَدْ تابَ اللهُ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ وَالْمُهاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصارِ. (Certainly Allah has relented toward the Prophet and the Emigrants and the Helpers) In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Majma', narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): They both read it as لَقَدْ تابَ اللهُ بالنّبيّ على المهاجرين (Certainly Allah has relented through the Prophet toward the Emigrants). And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed thus. And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Aban ibn Taghlib: I said to him, "O son of the Messenger of Allah, the common people (العامّة) do not recite as you have it." He said: "How do they recite, O Aban?" I said: "I said that they recite: لَقَدْ تابَ اللهُ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ وَالْمُهاجِرِينَ وَالْأَنْصارِ." So he said: "Woe to them! And what sin did the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commit that Allah should relent from it? Allah only relented through him toward his community (أمّته)." الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ فِي ساعَةِ الْعُسْرَةِ (1) (who followed Him in the hour of hardship) Al-Qummi: In the story of Tabuk, they are Abu Dharr, Abu Khaythama, and Umayrah ibn Wahb, who lagged behind Him and then caught up with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He said: And a group of people of steadfastness (ثبات) and insight (بصائر) lagged behind the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, whom no doubt or suspicion afflicted, but they said, "We will catch up with the Messenger of Allah." Among them was Abu Khaythama (2), and he was strong. He had two wives and two arbors (عريشتان). His two wives had sprinkled (water upon) (3) his arbor (عريشته) (4). He said: "No, by Allah, this is not justice (إنصاف).
