The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم—Allah has forgiven him what preceded of his sin and what followed—has gone out in the heat (الضّحّ) (5) and the wind, carrying weapons, striving in the way of Allah, while Abu Khaythama, who is strong, sits in his arbor with two beautiful women. No, by Allah, this is not justice." Then he took his she-camel, fastened his saddle upon her, and caught up with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. The people looked at a rider on the road and informed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of that. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Let it be Abu Khaythama." And it was Abu Khaythama who approached and informed the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم of what had happened, so He rewarded him well and prayed for him. As for Abu Dharr, he lagged behind the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for three days because his camel was emaciated (أعجف). He caught up after three days, and his camel stopped on him on part of the road, so he left it and carried his clothes on his back. When the day rose high, the Muslims looked at a person approaching. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Let it be Abu Dharr." They said: "It is Abu Dharr." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Reach him with water, for he is thirsty." So they reached him with water. Abu Dharr arrived before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and he had a waterskin (اداوة) containing water. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Abu Dharr, you have water, yet you are thirsty?" He said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you. I reached a rock upon which was rainwater. I tasted it, and behold, it was sweet and cold. I said: 'I will not drink it until my beloved, the Messenger of Allah, drinks it.'" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Abu Dharr, may Allah have mercy upon you. You shall live alone, and you shall die alone, and you shall be resurrected alone, and you shall enter Paradise alone. A people from Iraq shall be blessed through you, taking charge of your washing (غسلك), preparation (تجهيزك), and burial (دفنك)." In al-Jawami': And الْعُسْرَةِ (the hardship) refers to their condition in the Battle of Tabuk. Ten men would take turns riding a single camel. Their provisions were weevil-infested barley (الشعير المسوس), worm-eaten dates (التّمر المدوّد), and rancid fat (الأهالة) (1). The severity reached the point where two men would divide a single date, and sometimes a group would suck on it to drink water afterward (2). They were in the intense heat of summer (حمازة القيظ) and in severe distress due to drought (القحط) and scarcity of water. مِنْ بَعْدِ ما كادَ يَزِيغُ قُلُوبُ فَرِيقٍ مِنْهُمْ (after the hearts of a part of them had almost swerved) [Swerving] from steadfastness upon faith and from following the Messenger in that campaign. It was recited as tazīgh (تزيغ) with the tā’ (التاءِ). It is said that a group of them intended to turn back from their campaign without seeking permission, but Allah protected them until they proceeded. Al-Qummi: There was a man with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم at Tabuk called al-Mudarrab (المضرّب) due to the multitude of blows (ضرباته) that afflicted him at Badr and Uhud. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "Count the people of the army for me." So he counted them and said: "They are twenty-five thousand men, excluding the slaves (العبيد) and the followers (التّبّاع)." He said: "Count the believers (المؤمنين)." So he said: "Twenty-five men." ثُمَّ تابَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّهُ بِهِمْ رَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ (Then He relented toward them. Indeed, He is Rauf, Merciful to them.) He overtook them with His compassion (رأفته) and His mercy (رحمته).

(1) This is the difficulty of the matter. Jabir said: It means the hardship of provisions (الزّاد), the hardship of mounts (الظّهر), and the hardship of water. What is meant by the hour of hardship (ساعة العسرة) is the time of hardship, because the word sā‘ah (السّاعة) applies to any time. (2) With the dotted khā’ (الخاء) having a fatḥah, the silent lower yā’ (الياء التحتانّية), the dotted thā’ (الثاء المثلّثة), the mīm, and the hā’. (3) Meaning, they requested to take them. (4) The ‘arīsh (العريش) is like a litter (الهودج), or what is trellised for grapevines, or a house used for shade. (5) Al-Ḍaḥḥ (الضحّ) is the sun. And their saying, "So-and-so came with al-ḍaḥḥ and al-rīḥ (الريح)," means with whatever the sun rose upon and whatever the wind blew over, meaning due to the abundance (الكثرة).

(١١٨) وَعَلَى الثَّلاثَةِ الَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا (118) And on the three who were left behind

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are Ka'b ibn Malik (كعب بن مالك), Murarah ibn al-Rabi' (ومرارة بن الربيع), and Hilal ibn Umayyah (وهلال ابن أميّة). In al-Majma' (المجمع), narrated from Imam al-Sajjad, Imam al-Baqir, and Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): They read it as khālafū (خالفُوا) (1). Al-Qummi (والقمّيّ) said that the Scholar (العالم) (peace be upon him) said: It was only revealed as وَعَلَى الثَّلاثَةِ الَّذِينَ ** khālafū (خالَفُوا). If they had been khullifū (خلّفوا) (left behind), there would have been no blame (عتب) upon them. In al-Kafi (الكافي) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If they had been khullifū (left behind), they would have been in a state of obedience.

حَتَّى إِذا ضاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ بِما رَحُبَتْ ** until, when the earth became narrow for them, for all its breadth,**

Meaning: despite its vastness (سعتها). This is a metaphor (مثل) for their confusion (حيرتهم) regarding their situation, as if they could not find a place of rest (موضع قرار) on the earth.

وَضاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنْفُسُهُمْ ** and their own souls became narrow for them,**

Meaning: their hearts (قلوبهم) became narrow due to excessive loneliness (الوحشة) and grief (الغمّ).

وَظَنُّوا وعَلِمُوا أَنْ لا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ اللهِ ** and they knew for certain that there was no refuge from Allah**

And they knew for certain (وَظَنُّوا وعَلِمُوا) that there was no refuge from Allah (from the wrath (سخط) of Allah)

إِلَّا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تابَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** except toward Him. Then He relented toward them**

except toward Him. Then He relented toward them (Then He returned to them with acceptance (بالقبول)).

In al-Ma'ani (المعاني), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the granting of pardon (الاقالة).

لِيَتُوبُوا ** that they might repent.**

that they might repent (that they might return to their original state (حالتهم الأولى)).

إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ ** Indeed, Allah is the All-relenting, the All-merciful.**

Indeed, Allah is the All-relenting, the All-merciful (for the one who repents, even if he returns [to sin] a hundred times in a day). The verification of the meaning of repentance (التّوبة) from Allah and from the servant has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah.

Al-Qummi (والقمّيّ): [Narrated] concerning the story of the Battle of Tabuk (غزوة تبوك). A group of hypocrites (المنافقين) and a group of insightful believers (مؤمنين مستبصرين) who were not suspected of hypocrisy had stayed behind from the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). Among them were Ka'b ibn Malik the poet (كعب بن مالك الشّاعر), Murarah ibn al-Rabi' (ومرارة بن الرّبيع), and Hilal ibn Umayyah al-Waqifi (وهلال بن أميّة الواقفي).

When Allah relented toward them, Ka'b said: "I was never stronger than I was at the time when the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) set out for Tabuk. And I never had two riding beasts gathered for me except on that day. So I kept saying, 'I will go out tomorrow, I will go out the day after tomorrow, for I am strong.' And I delayed (توانيت) and remained behind (2) after the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) departed for days. I would enter the market but not fulfill any need. Then I met Hilal ibn Umayyah and Murarah ibn al-Rabi', who had also stayed behind. We agreed to go early to the market, but we did not fulfill any need. We kept saying, 'We will go out tomorrow and the day after tomorrow,' until the news of the return of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) reached us.

"Then we regretted it. When the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) arrived, we went out to meet Him to congratulate Him on His safe return. We greeted Him with salām, but He did not return our greeting. He turned away from us. We greeted our brothers, but they did not return our greeting. This reached our families, so they cut off speaking to us. We would attend the mosque, but no one would greet us or speak to us.

"Then our wives came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: 'Your displeasure with our husbands has reached us. Should we separate from them?'

"The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Do not separate from them, but they must not approach you.'

"When Ka'b ibn Malik and his two companions saw what had befallen them, he said: 'What keeps us in Medina when the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) does not speak to us, nor do our brothers or our families? Come, let us go out to this mountain and remain there until Allah relents toward us or we die.'

"So they went out to the tail (ذناب) of a mountain near Medina. They used to fast, and their families would bring them food, place it aside, and then turn away from them without speaking to them. They remained in this state for many days, weeping night and day, praying to Allah to forgive them. When the matter became prolonged for them, Ka'b said to them: 'O people, Allah is displeased with us, and His Messenger is displeased with us. Some of us are displeased with us, and our brothers are displeased with us, and our families are displeased with us. No one speaks to us. Why should we not be displeased with one another?'

"So they separated during the night and swore that none of them would speak to his companion until he died or Allah relented toward him. They remained in this state for three days, each of them in a corner of the mountain, neither seeing nor speaking to his companion.

"When it was the third night, and the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was in the house of Umm Salamah, their repentance was revealed to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). He said: حَتَّى إِذا ضاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ بِما رَحُبَتْ (until, when the earth became narrow for them, for all its breadth) — since the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), nor their brothers, nor their families spoke to them, Medina became narrow for them until they left it. وَضاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنْفُسُهُمْ ** (and their own souls became narrow for them) — since they swore that none of them would speak to the other, so they separated. And Allah relented toward them when He knew the sincerity of their intentions (صدق نيّاتهم).

footnote (١) The *Ahālah* (*الأهالة*) is every fat used for seasoning food. *Al-Sankhah* (*السّنخة*) (with an undotted *sīn*, *nūn*, and dotted *khā*) is the smell. And *humāzat al-qayẓ* (*حمازة القيظ*) (with an undotted *ḥā* and *zāy*) is its intensity. "From him, may Allah have mercy on him."

footnote (٢) Meaning: the changed water (*الماء المتغيّر*) m.

footnote (١) And in the other narration of *al-Ayyashi* and *al-Kafi*: That **الثَّلاثَةِ ** (the three) are Uthman and his two companions, that Allah imposed fear upon them, so they did not hear the sound of an unbeliever or the rattling of a stone except that they said, "We have been reached." So Allah pardoned them, but they did not repent. Perhaps this is an interpretation (*تأويل*) of the verse and an application (*إجراء*) of it to them. "From him, may Allah have mercy on him."

footnote (٢) *Al-Wanā* (*الونى*) (like *fatā*) is fatigue (*التّعب*) and weakness (*الفترة*), the opposite [of strength]. And it is extended (*يمدّ*). *Wanā* (*ونى*) *yanī* (*يني*) *wanīyan* (*ونياً*) and *wunīyan* (*وُنياً*), and *wuniya* (*وُنِيَ*) *wunyatan* (*ونيةً*) and *wanan* (*ونىً*). And *awwānahu* (*واوناه*). And *tawānā* (*توانى*) is him [being weak]. And a she-camel (*ناقة*) is *wāniyah* (*وآنية*), weak (*فاترة*), exhausted (*طليح*) q.

(١١٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَكُونُوا مَعَ الصَّادِقِينَ. (119) O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and be with the truthful.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "He means us (إيّانا)." And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "The truthful ones (الصادقون) are the Imams (peace be upon them), and the veracious ones (الصّدّيقون) are those who obey them."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Be with the Family of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace)."

And al-Qummi said: "They are the Imams (peace be upon them)."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), that He said: In an assembly of the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار) during the days of Uthman's caliphate: "I ask you by Allah, do you know that when this verse was revealed, Salman said: 'O Messenger of Allah, is this verse general or specific?' He replied: 'As for those commanded, they are the general body of believers who were commanded with this. But as for the truthful ones (الصّادقون), they are specific to my brother and my successors (أوصيائي) after him until the Day of Resurrection.' They said: 'O Allah, yes.'"

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited: "of the truthful ones (من الصَّادِقِينَ)."

ما كانَ لِأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُمْ مِنَ الْأَعْرابِ أَنْ يَتَخَلَّفُوا عَنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ وَلا يَرْغَبُوا بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ** (120) It is not for the people of Madinah and the Bedouins around them to hold back from Allah’s Apostle, nor to prefer their own lives to his life. Rather, it is incumbent upon them to accompany him through hardship (الْبَأْسَاءِ) and distress (الضَّرَّاءِ), and to endure the difficulties alongside him with eagerness and vigor, just as Abu Dharr and Abu Khaythama did. ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ لا يُصِيبُهُمْ ظَمَأٌ ** That is because neither thirst (nor) any degree of thirst (الْعَطَش) وَلا نَصَبٌ ** nor fatigue (nor) weariness (تَعَب) nor hunger (مَخْمَصَةٌ) (meaning) starvation (مَجَاعَة) فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** nor starvation afflicts them in the way of Allah, (meaning) on the path of Jihad (الْجِهَاد) وَلا يَطَؤُنَ ** nor do they tread (meaning) they do not step with their feet, nor with the hooves of their horses, nor the feet (أَخَاف) of their mounts (رَوَاحِلِهِم) مَوْطِئاً ** any ground whose treading upon it enrages the disbelievers (الْكُفَّارَ) and constricts their chests due to their movement (تَصَرُّفِهِم) in their land وَلا يَنالُونَ مِنْ عَدُوٍّ نَيْلاً ** nor do they gain any gain from an enemy, (meaning) by killing, or capturing, or plundering إِلَّا كُتِبَ لَهُمْ بِهِ عَمَلٌ صالِحٌ but that a righteous deed (عَمَلٌ صَالِحٌ) is written for them thereby, and they become deserving of the reward (الثَّوَاب) from Allah. إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ.** Indeed Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous.

(١٢١) وَلا يُنْفِقُونَ نَفَقَةً صَغِيرَةً وَلا كَبِيرَةً وَلا يَقْطَعُونَ وادِياً ** (nor do they spend any expenditure, small or large, nor do they cross a valley) a land/region (أرضاً) in their journey. And the valley (الوادي) is every open space (منفرج) through which the torrent (السيل) flows, so it became common to mean the land/region (الأرض). إِلَّا كُتِبَ لَهُمْ ** (but it is written down for them) that expenditure and the crossing of the valley, so that Allah may reward them for that **أَحْسَنَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (with the best of what they used to do) the reward of their best deeds, or the best reward for their deeds.

(١٢٢) وَما كانَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ لِيَنْفِرُوا كَافَّةً ** (122) The faithful should not go forth all together. And it is not proper for them that they should go forth all together for the purpose of battle (غزو) or seeking knowledge (طلب علم), just as it is not proper for them that they should hold back all together. فَلَوْ لا نَفَرَ مِنْ كُلِّ فِرْقَةٍ مِنْهُمْ (Why does not a group from every troop of them go forth) So why did not a group go forth from every large community, such as a tribe (قبيلة) or the people of a town (أهل بلدة), طائِفَةٌ ** a small group, لِيَتَفَقَّهُوا فِي الدِّينِ (to gain religious knowledge,) so that they may undertake the effort (يتكلّفُوا) to gain deep understanding (الفقاهة) in it, and endure (1) the hardships (مشاق) of acquiring it. وَلِيُنْذِرُوا قَوْمَهُمْ إِذا رَجَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ ** (and to warn their people when they return to them,) In this is an indication that the purpose of the one seeking deep understanding (المتفقه) should be to make himself upright (2) and to make others upright (ويقيم), not to elevate himself above people (الترفع على الناس) or to spread out in the lands (التبسط في البلاد). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَحْذَرُونَ ** (so that they may beware?) intending that they beware of what they are warned against.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to him that some people narrate that the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The disagreement (اختلاف) of my community is a mercy (رحمة)." He said: "They have spoken truthfully." It was said: "If their disagreement is a mercy, then their agreement (اجتماعهم) is a punishment (عذاب)?" He said: "It is not as you and they suppose. He only meant the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَلَوْ لا نَفَرَ مِنْ كُلِّ فِرْقَةٍ (Why does not a group from every troop of them go forth) [the rest of] the Ayah. So, He commanded them to go forth to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and travel to him (ويختلفوا إليه) to learn, and then return to their people to teach them. He only meant their going forth from the lands (البلدان), not a disagreement (اختلافاً) concerning the religion of Allah (دين الله). The religion is but one."

In al-Kafi, it was said to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "If an event occurs concerning the Imam (الإِمام), what should the people do?" He said: "Where is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَلَوْ لا نَفَرَ مِنْ كُلِّ فِرْقَةٍ ** [the rest of] the Ayah?" It was asked: "What is their condition (حالهم)?" He said: "They are excused (في عذر) as long as they are seeking [knowledge], and those who await them are excused until their companions return to them."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): [A narration] similar in meaning.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This was when the people became numerous, so He commanded that a group (طائفة) from them should go forth and a group should remain behind for deep understanding (للتفقه), and that the battle (الغزو) should be in turns (نوباً).

I say: This means that a group (طائفة) should remain with the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم for deep understanding (للتفقه) and to warn those who have gone forth (النافِرة). Thus, the going forth (النفر) is for battle (للغزو) and the remaining behind (القعود) is for deep understanding (للتفقّه).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Gain deep understanding (تفقهّوا) in the religion (الدين), for whoever among you does not gain deep understanding in the religion is a Bedouin (اعرابي). Indeed, Allah says in His Book: لِيَتَفَقَّهُوا فِي الدِّينِ وَلِيُنْذِرُوا قَوْمَهُمْ إِذا رَجَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ (to gain religious knowledge, and to warn their people when they return to them)."

(1) The verb جشم الأمر (jashama al-amr) (like سمع sami'a), jashman (جشما) and jashāmah (جشامة), means to undertake it despite hardship (تكلّفه على مشقة), like tajashshumuhu (تجشّمه).

(2) Meaning, he makes himself upright (يستقيم نفسه) and makes others upright (ويقيم غيره).

(١٢٣) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ يَلُونَكُمْ مِنَ الْكُفَّارِ ** (123) O you who have faith! Fight the infidels who are near you They were commanded to fight the nearest of them, then the next nearest, similar to [the verse]: وَأَنْذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ الْأَقْرَبِينَ ** (And warn your nearest kinsfolk). For the nearest is more deserving of compassion (الشفقة) and reform (الاستصلاح).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: الدّيلم and القمّيّ [said]: It is obligatory upon every people to fight those who are near them, among those who are close to the Imam (الإمام), and they must not pass beyond that location. وَلْيَجِدُوا فِيكُمْ غِلْظَةً (and let them find harshness in you) meaning severity (شدة) and patience (صبراً) in fighting.

القمّيّ [said]: Meaning, be harsh towards them in speech and killing. وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الْمُتَّقِينَ ** (and know that Allah is with the Godwary) through protection (الحراسة) and assistance (الإِعانة).

وَإِذا ما أُنْزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَمِنْهُمْ ** (124) When a surah is sent down, some of them say, [The phrase فَمِنْهُمْ (some of them) refers to] the hypocrites (المنافقين). مَنْ يَقُولُ ** (some of them say,) [They say this] out of denial (إنكاراً) and mockery (واستهزاء): أَيُّكُمْ زادَتْهُ هذِهِ ** ('Which of you did this increase) [meaning] this surah (السورة) إِيماناً فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَزادَتْهُمْ إِيماناً ** (in faith?' As for those who have faith, it increases them in faith,) [This increase is] through the increase of knowledge (العلم) resulting from reflecting upon the surah (تدبّر السورة) and the joining of faith in it and in what it contains. وَهُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ ** (and they rejoice.) [They rejoice] at its revelation, because it is the cause of the increase of their perfection (كمالهم) and the elevation of their ranks (درجاتهم).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: And this is a refutation of those who claim that faith (الإيمان) neither increases nor decreases.

In al-Kafi (الكافي) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, obligated faith (الإيمان) upon the limbs (جوارح) of the son of Adam, divided it among them, and distributed it within them. Then He (peace be upon him and his family) explained that. [Someone] asked: "I have understood the decrease (نقصان) and completeness (تمام) of faith, so from where does its increase (زيادته) come?" He (peace be upon him) said: "The saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَإِذا ما أُنْزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَقُولُ (When a surah is sent down, some of them say...) [the rest of] the Ayah. And He said: وَزِدْناهُمْ هُدىً (and We increased them in guidance). If all of it were one (واحداً), with no increase or decrease in it, then none of them would have superiority (فضل) over another, nor would the blessings (النّعم) be equal in it, nor would people be equal, and distinction (التفضيل) would be nullified. However, through the completeness (تمام) of faith, the believers entered Paradise (الجنّة). And through the increase (الزيادة) in faith, the believers are distinguished by ranks (بالدرجات) before Allah. And through the decrease (النقصان), the negligent (المفرّطون) entered the Fire (النار)." Further clarification (زيادة بيان) regarding this meaning has already passed in Surat al-Anfal (سورة الأنفال).

(١٢٥) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ فَزادَتْهُمْ رِجْساً إِلَى رِجْسِهِمْ (But as for those in whose hearts is a sickness, it adds defilement to their defilement) Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: doubt (شكاً) added to their doubt (شكهم). **وَماتُوا وَهُمْ كافِرُونَ ** (and they die while they are faithless.) And that became firmly established in them until they died upon it.

(١٢٦) أَوَلا يَرَوْنَ أَنَّهُمْ يُفْتَنُونَ فِي كُلِّ عامٍ مَرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ لا يَتُوبُونَ وَلا هُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (١٢٦) (126) Do they not see that they are tried once or twice every year, yet they do not repent nor do they take admonition?

أَوَلا يَرَوْنَ ** Do they not see It refers to the hypocrites (المنافقين). أَنَّهُمْ يُفْتَنُونَ ** that they are tried They are tested (يبتلون) with various kinds of afflictions (أصناف البليّات), or by fighting (بالجهاد) alongside the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), so they witness (فيعاينون) the signs (الآيات) that become manifest upon them. And al-Qummi (says): they fall ill (يمرضون). فِي كُلِّ عامٍ مَرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ لا يَتُوبُونَ ** once or twice every year, yet they do not repent from their hypocrisy (نفاقهم). وَلا هُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ ** nor do they take admonition They do not take heed (لا يعتبرون).

(١٢٧) وَإِذا ما أُنْزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ نَظَرَ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلى بَعْضٍ ** (127) When a surah is sent down, they look at one another, They winked at one another with their eyes, denying it and mocking it, or out of rage due to what it contained regarding their faults. هَلْ يَراكُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ ** 'Does anyone see you?' Meaning, they say: هَلْ يَراكُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ ** (Does anyone see you?) among the Muslims if you stand up and depart, for we cannot endure listening to it. And they exchanged glances, consulting one another on planning their exit and slipping away. If no one saw them, they stood up, and if someone saw them, they remained seated. ثُمَّ انْصَرَفُوا (Then they departed), they dispersed for fear of disgrace. صَرَفَ اللهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ (Allah has turned away their hearts) from faith and finding comfort in it, through abandonment (بالخذلان). And al-Qummi (said): (He turned them) from the truth to falsehood, due to their choosing falsehood over truth. It is said that it might also imply a supplication (a curse). بِأَنَّهُمْ ** (because they are) because they are **قَوْمٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** (a people who do not understand) due to their poor understanding and lack of reflection.

(١٢٨) لَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ ** (128) Certainly there has come to you an Apostle from among yourselves. [He is] of your kind (جنسكم), an Arab (عربيّ). Al-Qummi [said]: [He is] like you in creation. He said: And it is read as مِنْ أَنْفَسِكُمْ (from the most precious of you), meaning: from the noblest (أشرفكم) of you. In al-Jawami', it was said that this is the reading (قراءة) of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and Fatimah. عَزِيزٌ عَلَيْهِ ** grievous to him is your distress. [It is] severe and difficult (شديد شاق) for him, that which distresses you (عَنِتُّمْ), [meaning] your distress (عنتكم) and your encountering the disliked thing (المكروه). And Al-Qummi [said]: [It is] that which you denied (أنكرتم) and rejected (جحدتم). حَرِيصٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** He is eager for your well-being. [He is eager] for your faith (إيمانكم) and the rectification of your affairs (صلاح شأنكم), so that none of you departs from attaining happiness (الاستسعاد) through the religion He brought. [He is] gentle (رَؤُفٌ) and merciful (رَحِيمٌ) to the believers (بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ), whether they are from among you or from others besides you.

(١٢٩) فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا ** (129) But if they turn away, from believing in you, فَقُلْ حَسْبِيَ اللهُ ** (say, 'Allah is sufficient for me.') Seek help (استعن) through Allah, for He will suffice you concerning their affair and grant you victory over them. لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ** (There is no god except Him; in Him I have put my trust,) so I hope for none other than Him, and I fear none except Him, and He is رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ.** (the Lord of the Great Throne.')

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It means] the Great Dominion (الملك العظيم).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): رَسُولٌ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ ** (a Messenger from among yourselves) He said: [This refers] to us. عَزِيزٌ عَلَيْهِ ما عَنِتُّمْ ** (It grieves Him that you should suffer hardship) He said: [This refers] to us. حَرِيصٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (He is eager for your well-being) He said: [This refers] to us. بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (and is gentle and merciful toward the faithful.) He said: The believers share with us in this fourth [quality], and three [qualities] are for us. And in another narration: So, three-quarters of it are for us, and one-quarter is for our followers (شيعتِنا).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Thus did Allah the Almighty reveal: Indeed, there has come to us رَسُولٌ مِنْ ** (a Messenger from) ourselves (أنفسنا), عَزِيزٌ عَلَيْهِ ما ** (It grieves Him that) our hardship (عنتنا), حَرِيصٌ ** (He is eager) for us (علينا), بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ (gentle and merciful toward the faithful).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surat al-Anfal and Surat al-Bara'ah every month, hypocrisy (نفاق) shall never enter him, and he shall be among the followers (شيعةِ) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And al-Ayyashi added: And on the Day of Resurrection, he shall eat from the tables (موائد) of Paradise with his followers (شيعته) until the people finish the reckoning (الحساب).
